Flop Qd6d9c completely misses: no pair, no straight draw (gutshot to 10 is weak/non-existent), no flush draw (spades not on board), pure air with minimal backdoors. Board favors villain's button range heavily (Qx, 97s, AdXd, pairs). OOP with nothing, no fold equity to bet (villain short ~790 into 700 pot, can easily call/shove wide), standard check-fold to preserve stack. Deep effective stacks but villain short so he'll bet most of his range for value/protection; folding saves chips for better spots over 150 hands.

I'm holding 8h9d and hit middle pair (nines) on the Qd 6d 9c flop. With only 790 chips remaining, I'm in push-fold territory and need to apply pressure rather than play passively. Elon's check indicates potential weakness - he could have a weak hand, a draw, or be slowplaying, but checking is generally a sign of vulnerability here. My pair of 9s is a reasonable holding on this flop. Betting 500 accomplishes several objectives: (1) it applies significant pressure with ~71% of remaining stack committed, (2) it likely wins the pot immediately given Elon's checked action, (3) if called, I'm roughly 50-50 or slightly ahead in a likely race/contest, and (4) it's appropriate sizing given my short stack situation. Checking would be passive and wasteful - I'd be giving a free card on a two-tone board where Elon could improve. The aggressive line with a short stack is optimal here.

Flop completely misses Js8s: no pair, no flush draw (spades vs diamond-heavy board), weak gutshot at best to T for marginal straight, pure air with backdoors only. Preflop call was speculative OOP vs wide button range, but board favors villain heavily (Qx, 9x, 76s, AdXd, pairs). OOP check-fold standard. Pot 1200, call 500 (~2.4:1 direct odds), but Claude has only 290 behind post-bet, so pot becomes ~1700 with SPR <1; he'll likely shove any turn or check weak hands. No showdown value, minimal fold equity to raise (short stack calls wide), better to preserve deep stack for 92 hands remaining rather than risk 500+ more with nothing vs polarized value range.

On the button with KTo offsuit after SB (Minni) folds and BB (Claude) checks. This is heads-up effectively, with position and a strong broadway hand. Claude is short-stacked (~14BB effective), so raising steals the dead SB money and BB frequently, or gets value from worse. KTo blocks AA/KK/QQ and plays well postflop in position. Standard raise sizing to 300 (3x BB) balances steal/value.

I’m on the small blind with 2s5s facing a button open to 300. Pot is 450 and it costs me 250 more to call. This is a very weak, low-suited hand with poor showdown value and I’ll be out of position against the raiser; the button’s opening range will dominate most of my holdings. The big blind is a short stack (~1390) who could shove or fold, which cuts my implied odds. A 3-bet here would be overly ambitious with such garbage; calling is marginal and leads to difficult postflop decisions. Preserve the stack and fold to pick better spots later.

AJ is a premium hand and warrants a call in heads-up play. With 1,390 chips and only needing 200 to call against Elon's 300 raise, the pot odds are favorable at 2.25:1. This hand plays well in position post-flop and has strong equity against Elon's typical button raising range. Calling preserves chip flexibility while giving me a chance to win the pot with one of the best non-premium hands.

I hold AJ on a favorable A-7-3 flop, giving me a pair of aces with top kicker. This is a strong, leading hand in this heads-up situation against Elon.

Analysis:
- Hand strength: Top pair with top kicker is the likely best hand here. Elon's button raising range includes many weaker holdings.
- Stack consideration: With 1,190 chips remaining after the flop, I'm in a short-stack situation but not yet desperate. I need to build the pot with strong hands.
- Bet sizing: A 400-chip bet represents approximately 60% of the pot (650), which is a standard value bet. This size:
  - Extracts value from weaker pairs (AK, KQ, small pairs like 77)
  - Doesn't over-commit my stack unnecessarily
  - Gives Elon a reasonable decision point
  
- Positioning: While Elon is in position post-flop, this is a strong enough hand that I need to take the initiative and define the action. Checking might allow Elon to check through or take control with his stack advantage.

With proper odds and good hand strength, betting is the value-maximizing play here.

Claude is extremely short-stacked with only 790 chips left after his flop donk bet of 400 into a 650 pot. My KToo has solid blockers to strong Ax combos (Kh blocks top pair), backdoor potential, and position. His donk range includes Ax for value but also weaker hands, bluffs, or draws he wants to rep Ax with. Raising maximizes fold equity against his marginal holdings while denying equity. If he jams his last 790, pot odds are ~3:1 (pot ~2250 into 790), and I have decent equity (~35-45%) vs his polarized jam range. Calling is loose without a hand, folding is too weak to a small bet OOP from shorty vs button raise. Raise to 1200 total applies perfect pressure.

Top pair top kicker (AJ) on A73 flop is a strong leading hand in heads-up play. With 790 chips remaining against Elon's 1200 raise, I'm committed to call all-in. Elon's button raising and re-raising range includes many hands I beat (AQ, AK, smaller pairs, bluffs). Even against premium holdings like AA, I have 12 outs (three remaining aces and four jacks visible to run twice, plus straight possibilities with the remaining cards). At 2.85:1 pot odds and short-stacked, this is a necessary spot to make a stand with one of my strongest hands. The all-in call gives me maximum equity realization.

Button with Qc7s is marginal but in 3-handed play I can open wider. Raise to 300 (≈3x the BB) to take the initiative and apply pressure to the short SB (Claude, 2980) while still keeping a reasonable stack-to-pot ratio vs Elon. This sizing is consistent with table tendencies and gives good fold equity; if called I'll play postflop in position — c-bet on favorable boards, check/defend more cautiously on dry misses. I will fold to large 3-bets or an isolated shove from Claude since Q7o is poorly situated against polarized all-in ranges.

A3o is a marginal hand that's dominated by most raising ranges and vulnerable to all overcards. Being out of position with a short stack against Minni's button raise is not worth the commitment. Folding preserves chips for better opportunities with premium hands. The weak kicker ensures that even if an ace hits, I'll face difficult decision-making postflop.

Preflop in the big blind facing a button raise to 300 from Minni after SB folds. My hand is 3h5s, a very weak offsuit hand with poor connectivity, low playability, and minimal showdown value. Pot is 450, calling 200 offers about 2.25:1 odds, but equity against Minni's button opening range (wide but still favoring her) is too low (~25-30% at best), and postflop OOP with this hand is disastrous due to reverse implied odds and difficulty realizing equity. No fold equity to justify a 3-bet. Standard fold to preserve stack and avoid speculative calls OOP with trash.

5-7 offsuit is a weak holding that doesn't warrant investment, even from the button with positional advantage. While I'm getting 2.5:1 odds (150 to call 100), my actual hand equity against two opponents is poor and the hand has minimal showdown value. With a relatively short stack of 2980 chips (~30 big blinds), I should be selective and preserve chips for premium or strong speculative hands. This is a clear fold.

Preflop in the small blind facing a fold from the button, leaving heads-up vs BB. 7h Td is a weak offsuit hand with poor connectivity and playability out of position post-flop. Completing the small blind gives great pot odds (3:1), but the hand has low equity against a calling range and struggles OOP on most boards. Raising is not justified with this trash hand. Folding preserves stack for better opportunities in a deep-stack tournament with 25k chips (250BB effective). Standard play is to fold marginal offsuit hands like this in SB vs BB.

I am on the button with 8h3d, a very weak offsuit hand with no connectivity, suited potential, or high card value. My stack is deep at 25k, but this hand has poor playability postflop and low equity against calling ranges. Even on the button, standard play is to fold trash like this to avoid difficult spots. Blinds may defend, especially Claude who is shorter at ~29BB, but risking chips here isn't +EV long-term. Prioritizing hand selection to maximize stack over 150 hands.

I'm in the small blind with 5♦6♠ facing only the big blind (Claude, 2,880). With the button folded this is effectively a heads-up steal spot and I have a large stack (11,930) vs his ~29BB. 5-6o is weak, but open-raising a wide range here is +EV — it picks up the blinds and lets me play the flop with initiative and (likely) position. I choose a standard-sized open rather than limp: raise to 300 total. This size pressures Claude's marginal defenses but keeps the pot manageable; if he 3-bets big or shoves I can fold this hand, and if he calls I can continue postflop with a clear plan. Calling 50 is too passive given the stack dynamics and opening frequency at this table.

I'm holding AKs (Ace-King of spades), one of the strongest possible non-paired hands. From the big blind facing a 300 raise from Minni, I have excellent equity. With a relatively short stack of 2880, I need to build pots with premium hands rather than play passively. The pot is 400 and I'm getting favorable odds to call, but raising is superior because: (1) AKs is best played aggressively pre-flop, (2) my short stack situation means I should leverage strong hands to build chips, (3) a raise to 900 applies meaningful pressure while still being reasonable relative to my stack size, and (4) this allows me to potentially take down the pot immediately or get it in with the best of it if called. A raise to 900 total represents about 31% of my stack, which is appropriate for a premium hand in this spot.

I opened to 300 as a wide steal with 5♦6♠, but Claude 3‑bet to 900 leaving only ~1,180 behind. Calling 600 into a 1,200 pot (~33% equity needed) with 5‑6o out of position is weak — I'm frequently dominated and will face a shove or tough decisions on later streets. 4‑betting as a bluff is risky here because he can commit his short stack and I don't want to gamble a big portion of my 11,680 on a speculative offsuit hand. Preserve stack and fold to the 3‑bet.

I’m on the button with J♦4♦ — a marginal but playable suited hand with position against two opponents. With pot 150 and only 100 to call, I prefer to take the initiative and open a wide stealing range from the button rather than letting the big blind see a cheap flop. A standard 3x-ish raise to 300 pressures Claude’s shorter stack (3230) and forces Elon to decide; if I face a large 3-bet (well above a single re-raise, e.g. ~700+), I’ll fold J4s and preserve my deep stack. If I get called, I’ll use position to c-bet favorable flops, control pot size on dry boards, and realise equity on backdoor diamond/straight textures.

Folding 2-10 offsuit from small blind against Minni's button raise. This is a weak hand that plays poorly out of position. While my stack is getting short, losing chips with marginal hands is worse than waiting for a better spot. Preserving chips for premium holdings is essential at this tournament stage.

Preflop in the big blind facing a button raise to 300 after SB folds. Pot is 450, need to call 200 more for odds of about 3.25:1. Hand is A7o, which blocks AA, AK, AQ and has good postflop playability with ace-high potential that dominates parts of button's wide opening range (suited connectors, broadway, weaker aces, etc.). Button opens very wide from this position, especially given Minni's aggressive history of frequent raises. Defending wide in BB is standard to realize equity OOP. Folding is too tight; 3-betting OOP risks playing a marginal ace out of position if called, and fold equity might not be great against an aggressive button. Calling realizes equity, controls pot size, and keeps us in with position denial postflop while leveraging implied odds with deep stacks (mine 25k, Minni 11k). Plan to play postflop cautiously, c-bet if good board, check raises or floats as texture warrants.

Flop is 9s-3d-5c, a dry, low, uncoordinated board that misses most of Minni's wide button opening range. My A7o gives me ace-high showdown value, which is often good against her missed draws, weak pairs, and bluffs. I block AA/AK/AQ, reducing her premium value hands, and have decent playability. OOP first to act in a 650 pot with deep stacks (mine 24.7k, her 11k). Donk betting is non-standard and invites raises; better to check and pot control, inducing bluffs from her aggressive c-bet heavy range (seen in prior hands like H5, H15, H25 where she bets flops). Plan: check-call small/medium c-bets (~300-500), check-fold to large overbets or check-raise if very strong bet sizing indicates value. This realizes equity while denying her free cards on blank turns. Folding is too tight given blockers and range advantage.

I opened wide on the button with J♦4♦ and got a single caller from the big blind. The flop 9♠-3♦-5♣ is very dry and misses a large portion of the caller’s calling range (many broadways, suited connectors and weak pairs). I only have an overcard (J) and a backdoor diamond possibility — no made hand — but positional advantage plus the dry texture makes this a textbook c-bet spot to take the pot down or charge marginal pairs. I’ll make a standard, controlled c-bet of 250 (≈35–40% pot): large enough to fold many of his missed hands and floats, but small enough not to bloated the pot with a marginal holding. Plan: take it down if he folds; if he calls I’ll re-evaluate on the turn and only commit further with a clear improvement (pair, strong diamond) or a good river plan. If he check-raises heavy I’ll fold — I don’t have the equity to get sticky.

Facing a small 250 bet (~1/3 pot) into a 650 pot on a dry 9s-3d-5c flop that heavily favors my checking range over her wide button opening range. A7o (As7c) holds ace-high equity, strong blockers to her AA/AK/AQ value hands, and decent showdown value against her frequent c-bets with air, weak pairs, or missed draws (as seen in her history: H15, H25, H16 where she fires small flops aggressively). OOP, check-folding is too tight given pot odds (900:250 ~3.6:1) and implied odds with deep stacks (my 24k vs her 11k). Raising as a bluff is suboptimal on uncoordinated board; better to call, pot control, induce further bluffs on turn, and realize equity. Plan: call small bet, evaluate turn texture—if blank/improver check-call again or check-fold large bets; if scary, consider check-raise if polarized.

Heads-up OOP vs Minni's button range after calling her small flop c-bet 250 into 650 on dry 9-3-5 rainbow. Pot now 1150 on turn 10s, which adds a spade flush draw (9s-Ts) and opens gutshot straight draws (e.g., J8, 76), making the board more coordinated and favoring her aggressive continuation range including floats, draws, and improved pairs. A<7>c holds ace-high showdown value against her many missed draws, weak 9x/5x/Tx, and bluffs (her history shows frequent small c-bets on flops like H15, H16, H25, H51, often with air or marginal). Strong blockers to top value (AA, AK, A9, A5) reduce her nuts, and my spade ace blocks flush draws somewhat. Stack depths (my 24.5k effective vs her 11k) offer implied odds if she bluffs further. Donking OOP is non-standard, invites raises from her value/draws, and bloats pot with marginal equity. Raising unseen is bluff-heavy on this texture. Check-call small/medium bets (~400-800, ~1/3-2/3 pot) realizes equity per prior plan; check-fold to large bets/overbets indicating strong value/draws. Check induces her bluffs/checks behind often on turn (as in H3, H5, H57 where opps check turns). Plan post-turn: if river blank/improver, value bet thin or check-call; if scary, check-fold. Folding pre-turn too tight given pot odds built-in and range advantage from BB defense.

I c-bet the dry 9♠-3♦-5♣ flop (250) and Elon just called. The turn T♠ is a higher card that helps his calling-range (T-x, broadways, two-pair/sets) and it also kills my backdoor diamond plan (I needed both turn+river diamonds). With J♦4♦ I have no pair, no meaningful draw and very little showdown equity. A third barrel here would be a pure bluff with limited fold equity against a caller of my flop bet, and risks bloating a pot I can’t realistically win at showdown. I’ll check to control the pot, see his action, and preserve stack. If he bets a small probe I’ll fold; if he checks river I can consider a delayed bluff on a favorable river (or a value bet if I improve).

River 9h pairs the top card (9s), creating possible two-pair combos (9x with T/5/3), trips (99, but unlikely given preflop action), or full houses unlikely. Board: 9s-3d-5c-Ts-9h remains low-coordinated with spade flush draw complete but my As blocks strong spades like KsQs, ATs. A7o holds ace-high showdown value against Minni's wide button range (missed draws like J8s, 76s, KQo floats; weak pairs 87o, QJo; bluffs she often reps with small flop c-bet then check-turn). Her flop c-bet 250/650 (~38% pot small, capping value), turn check after my check suggests medium strength (pairs without improvement, draws missed) rather than nuts (would bet turn for value/protection). History shows Minni checks turns behind when weak (H3, H4, H5, H13 river but similar passivity; H57 full check-downs), bets value/draws aggressively otherwise. OOP first to act in 1150 pot, effective stacks deep (her 11k covers multiple bets). Donk betting thin ace-high risks folding to her calls/raises from better aces/pairs while bloating pot OOP with ~60-70% equity vs her capped range (GTO sims suggest check OOP river with blocker ace-high on paired boards). Checking realizes equity, induces her bluffs/value bets thin (her aggression post-oak), pots control, and likely gets showdown as she checks back worse often (e.g., H10, H15 river checks). If she bets small/medium, call for value; large bet fold to polarization. No fold equity from bet on scary paired board. Optimal: check for pot control and max showdown value.

Board: 9♠-3♦-5♣-T♠-9♥. I hold J♦4♦ — no pair, no completed diamond draw, essentially air. I c-bet the dry flop and was called; both checked the turn and the river. Given Elon’s line (call flop → passive checks), his range is weighted to one-pair hands (9x, 5x, Txx), some showdownable broadways, and sticky pairs that will often call a river bluff. To make a bluff profitable here I’d need on the order of ~30–40% immediate fold equity for a half-pot bet — unlikely after he called the flop. Betting risks getting called by too many hands; checking preserves a healthy stack and concedes a small pot rather than spewing chips on a low-+EV bluff. Action: check to go to showdown (and fold to a river bet if he suddenly leads).

I'm on the button with K4o, a marginal but playable hand from this position. Both opponents are in the blinds. I'm getting 2.5:1 odds to call 100 chips, and my position advantage on the flop is valuable. My stack is short but not critically short (32 BB), so calling to see a flop from late position is the optimal play. This allows me to leverage position while preserving the option to fold if the flop is unfavorable.

9c6s is a trash hand, offsuit and poorly connected. In the small blind out of position against a button limp from Claude, who may have a wide range, and Minni yet to act in the big blind. Calling 50 OOP risks difficult post-flop spots with no initiative and poor playability. Folding preserves my deep stack (25k+) for better spots in this tournament with 150 hands total. No need to speculate or bluff-raise preflop with this holding.

I have A♦K♥ in the big blind facing a lone limp from the button (Claude). This is too strong to just check and give him a cheap flop — I want to isolate and take the initiative. Claude is relatively short (~31bb), so a sizable preflop raise will either win the pot now, induce a shove (where AK is often a correct call), or get me heads-up with a clear equity advantage postflop. I’ll raise to 900 total (putting 800 more in) — big enough to price out speculative hands and create fold equity without needlessly committing my deep stack. If he folds I scoop the 250 pot; if he calls or shoves I can proceed knowing AK has strong showdown equity.

Minni has raised to 900 from the big blind after I called 100. With K4o, a marginal hand, I'm facing 800 to call into a 1050 pot (2.31:1 odds). While the pot odds are passable at ~43%, several factors make folding correct: (1) K4o has poor equity against Minni's raising range, (2) I'm short-stacked at 3130 chips (~31 BB), committing over 25% to this marginal spot, (3) I'll be out of position postflop with a weak hand, and (4) Minni's raise from the BB after my button call indicates strength. With limited chips and marginal holdings, I should reserve my stack for premium spots or better odds. This is a clear fold.

Preflop on the button with Qd5c offsuit, a weak, uncoordinated hand with poor playability postflop. Pot is 150 after SB limp (Minni) and BB check (Claude), facing a call of 100 for 1.5:1 odds, but my hand has low equity multiway and is easily dominated by better queens or calling ranges. Claude's short stack (30BB) in BB might shove wide if raised, but this isn't a stealing spot worth risking with trash. Folding preserves stack for better opportunities in position.

I have 3♥5♠ in the small blind heads-up vs Claude (BB ~30bb). This is a very weak, OOP hand — not worth a raise — but the call is cheap: I need 50 more to see a flop into a ~200 chip pot (good pot odds). Plan is to complete and see the flop, keeping the pot small; fold to significant aggression (preflop 3-bet/shove or strong flop betting) since I have poor equity and position. If I connect with a pair or a decent draw I can proceed cautiously; otherwise I’ll concede the pot. Therefore I’ll call the 50 to complete to 100 total.

I'm in the Big Blind with 3s-2d (worst hand in poker) in a heads-up situation against Minni who called from the Small Blind. Elon has folded. With such a weak hand, there's no value in raising - I should keep the pot small and see what develops. My stack is reasonable at 3030 (30BB), so I'm not in desperate situation yet. Checking allows me to reach the flop cheaply and potentially improve, or fold if the board is bad. This is the prudent line with a trash hand in position.

Flop: 2♠ 7♣ K♦. I hold 3♥5♠ — I completely missed. No pair and no realistic draw (only extremely remote backdoor possibilities). I’m out of position versus Claude (BB ~30bb) and the pot is small (~200). Per my preflop plan I called cheaply to see a flop but intended to concede when I didn’t connect. Checking here controls the pot, preserves chips, and lets me fold to any meaningful aggression. Plan: check; if Claude bets I’ll fold to significant sizing (no reason to chase with almost no equity). If he checks back I’ll evaluate the turn for a free card or a better bluffing spot.

Checking with 3s-2d on a 2s-7c-Kd flop in a heads-up situation against a checking opponent. My hand is extremely weak (pair of 2s with 3 kicker), and with a 30BB stack, I cannot justify betting here. Minni's check indicates weakness or a trap - either way, I'm not strong enough to build the pot. Checking keeps the pot small, preserves chips, and allows me to see the turn and either improve or fold if she bets. This is proper bankroll management with a trash hand when short-stacked.

Board is 2♠ 7♣ K♦ 7♠; I hold 3♥5♠ — I completely missed. I have no pair and no realistic draw (only weak pair-outs to a 3 or 5 that still lose to a 7 or K). Claude has checked flop and turn (passive line) and the pot is small (~200) versus my deep stack. I want to control the pot and preserve chips — betting the turn as a bluff here is unnecessary and easily called by better hands. Plan: check and go to the river for a free card; fold to any meaningful bet on the river unless I hit a clean, strong hand or the board gives a highly believable bluff spot.

I'm holding 3s-2d with a pair of 2s on a board of 2s-7c-Kd-7s. This is an extremely weak hand - just a pair of deuces with a 3 kicker. Minni has checked twice (flop and turn), which indicates weakness or a draw that hasn't materialized. With my short stack of 3030 chips (30BB) in a tournament, I cannot afford to squander chips on marginal hands. Betting here serves little purpose - if Minni has a weak hand, she'll fold and I win a small pot; if she has something, I'm likely beat. Checking allows me to see the river for free and potentially win the hand if my pair of 2s is best at showdown. This is proper bankroll management when short-stacked - preserve chips for better spots and avoid unnecessary variance with a trash hand.

I missed the river with 3♥5♠ — no pair, no draw. Both players have been passive (checked flop and turn) and the pot is tiny (~200) relative to our stack. There’s almost no fold equity for a bluff against Claude’s likely checking/calling range (Kx, 7x, medium pairs), and betting here would risk chips for little expected gain. Per my preflop/turn plan I keep the pot small: check and take a free showdown. If Claude bets into me I will fold to meaningful sizing — no reason to invest further with essentially zero showdown equity.

River action with 3s-2d (pair of deuces) on board 2s-7c-Kd-7s-Th in heads-up against Minni. Minni has checked all three streets (flop, turn, river), indicating significant weakness - either a missed draw or genuinely weak holding. My pair of deuces with a 3 kicker is likely ahead given her passive line. The pot is only 200 chips and I'm 30BB deep (3030 chips), so chip preservation is critical. Betting here achieves little: if she folds, I win a small pot; if she calls, I'm likely beat and lose more chips. Checking allows me to reach showdown where my weak pair probably wins the hand. This is proper bankroll management in a short-stack tournament situation - extract value with showdown rather than risking additional chips with a marginal hand. Her checking line strongly suggests my pair of 2s is best.

I’m on the button with 8♠6♠ and deep stacks (≈112 BB). With both blinds posted and no raise, this is a good spot to take the initiative and open-raise — the hand plays well in position and can realize equity postflop. I’ll raise to 300 (≈3x BB) to discourage marginal calls from the small blind, isolate heads-up, and set up easier postflop decisions. If Elon 3-bets large I’ll be prepared to fold this speculative holding; if called I’ll use position to c-bet favorable flops and extract value or fold vs heavy action.

Folding 97o from SB. This is a weak off-suit hand that plays poorly out of position. With a short stack of 3,180 chips, calling would be speculative and put me in a tough spot post-flop when I miss (65% of the time). While the pot odds are borderline acceptable, positional disadvantage combined with hand weakness makes this an easy fold. Preserving stack for premium hands is the correct strategy at this stack depth.

In the big blind facing a button raise to 300 from Minni, my hand is J3 offsuit, which is extremely weak with no straight potential, no flush potential, and dominated by virtually her entire raising range. Defending this hand would be a significant mistake as my equity is poor, and I'm out of position post-flop. Even with pot odds of calling 200 into 450 (about 3.25:1), the hand plays terribly multiway or heads-up OOP. Standard fold to preserve stack and avoid difficult spots.

I'm holding Ts Ks (King-Ten suited), a strong broadway hand on the button with a short stack of 3,180 chips. Given my position and stack depth (~30 BB), this is an excellent spot to apply maximum pressure. King-Ten is a premium holding that dominates a wide range of hands and plays well against two opponents. With a short stack, I need to be aggressive and get chips in with quality hands. I'm pushing all-in to take down the pot immediately or get it in good if called. This is textbook short-stack poker from the button.

Hand #67, pre-flop as Small Blind with 2c5c, a very weak suited connector with poor playability and low equity against a strong Button raise range. Claude's raise to 3180 into a small blind/pot is large (about 31BB), suggesting premium hands or a steal, but calling out of position with this hand risks too much stack (3130 to call into 3330 pot for ~25% equity at best) with minimal fold equity post-flop. Minni in BB may defend, but 25s plays poorly multiway OOP. Prioritizing stack preservation in tournament with 150 hands total, easy fold to avoid spew.

Claude has shoved all‑in for 3,180 (stack now 0). I need to put in 3,080 more to call (making my total committed 3,180) into a pot of 3,330. That means I need roughly 3,080 / (3,330 + 3,080) ≈ 48% equity to break even. J♣8♦ is marginal, but against a short‑stack shove from the button Claude is likely shoving a wide, exploitable range (many pairs, broadways and weaker hands). The call is only ~27% of my 11,230 stack, and raising is pointless because he’s already all‑in. Given the pot odds, opponent’s likely wide shove range, and the chance to eliminate a short stack, this is a +EV call. I will call to try to pick up the pot.

Preflop in SB vs BB heads-up, with 62o, an extremely weak hand with no high card value, poor connectivity, and offsuit. Equity against any calling range is dismal, and while SB has a stealing opportunity, this is too weak even for that. Stacks are deep (25k+), so no need to gamble 50 chips on a fold equity bluff with trash. Folding preserves stack for better spots. Minni's history shows she defends BB variably but folding is standard GTO here.

Heads-up on the button (I’m the small blind) with 9♣6♦ — a marginal but playable hand in position. Against a single big blind I should be opening a wide range to apply pressure and take the initiative. I’ll raise to the minimum open (200 total) to steal the blind and keep the pot size manageable. Plan: fold to a sizable 3‑bet (this hand is often dominated), call small 3‑bets only with pot odds or if I see a clear postflop plan; if called I’ll use positional advantage to c‑bet favorable dry flops and control the pot on coordinated boards.

Heads-up preflop in BB vs SB/Button raise to 200 (2BB). My hand Q4o is offsuit Queen-high, marginal but with excellent pot odds: calling 100 more into 300 pot (~3:1, needing ~25% equity). Minni's button raises are wide (frequent small raises in history, e.g., Hands 51,53,54,60,66). Q4o has ~35-40% equity vs a typical 45-55% button open range, plus playability postflop with deep stacks (my 25k, her 14k). Defending wide in BB vs late position is standard to realize equity and deny her fold equity. Folding is too tight; 3-betting too strong for this hand. Call and see flop.

Heads-up on flop with Qc4h (Queen-high, offsuit, no pair, no draws) on a draw-heavy board 5d7d2c (flush draw, gutshots like 34/68). Minni checked from Button after wide preflop raise, indicating possible weakness (missed overcards/draws) or trap (sets/pairs unlikely). Pot 400, deep stacks. Q4 has minimal showdown value and poor playability as bluff—no strong blockers to draws/value hands. Donk betting risks folding out better but worse hands; called preflop to realize equity cheaply. Check back for pot control, see turn free, fold to aggression or value bet later streets if needed. Avoid bloating pot with trash.

I opened preflop to 200 and was called; on 5♦7♦2♣ I hold 9♣6♦ — no pair and only weak backdoor possibilities. As the preflop aggressor in position I have a range advantage and this flop is relatively dry and low, so a continuation bet will fold out a lot of unpaired hands and keep the pot manageable. I’ll make a half‑pot c‑bet (200) to apply pressure while risking little. Plan: if Elon folds I take it; if he calls I’ll slow down on the turn (check/control the pot) and only continue with improvement or very specific turn cards; if he check‑raises or gives strong resistance I’ll concede this bluff and fold.

Facing a 200 bet into 600 pot (half-pot) on a highly coordinated flop (5d7d2c) with Qc4h (queen-high, no pair, no draws, 0 equity realizations). Preflop defense was wide for BB realization/odds, but flop miss is complete—minimal showdown value, plays poorly vs range with flush/straight draws, pairs (22-77,54s etc.). Minni's button bet after check indicates polarized range (value like sets/pairs/top pair or bluffs/draws), but even vs bluffs, calling bloats pot OOP with trash, facing further aggression on turns/rivers likely improving her range. Stacks deep (25k me, 14k her), but folding marginal preflop defenders to postflop bets prevents spew; preserve stack for better spots. Check-fold achieved as planned when bet into.

Heads-up on the button/SB with 4c5c suited connectors. Excellent postflop playability, good implied odds with deep effective stacks (14k), and position advantage. BB range is wide, so this hand has solid equity (~40% vs typical defending range). Raising small takes initiative, builds a pot for multi-street play or steals blinds frequently. Limp/call is too passive; fold too tight. Standard small raise sizing for SB vs BB.

Heads-up in the big blind vs a 300 open from the button. I have Jc7c — a suited, connected hand that plays well postflop against a wide opening range. Pot is 400 and I need to put in 200 more (I already have 100 in as the BB), so calling to a total of 300 gives me good pot odds (~33% break-even) and strong implied odds with deep stacks. A small 3-bet to 500 would be too light and thin with this hand — I don’t want to bloat the pot out of position with a marginal holding. Plan: call, see the flop; continue aggressively if I hit club draw/pair/strong draw, otherwise fold to heavy action.

I called preflop with Jc7c to see a flop, but this Q-T-5 (5s Ts Qd) misses my hand — no pair and only a very weak backdoor club possibility. There are two spades on board which makes my hand vulnerable to opponent’s spade draws; I’m out of position and want to keep the pot manageable. With deep stacks I can realize equity if I improve, but I don’t want to bloat the pot OOP with a marginal holding. Plan: check and evaluate Elon’s action — call a small c-bet to see a turn, fold to large aggression, and only get aggressive if I pick up meaningful equity or showdown value.

Preflop raise with suited connectors from SB got called by BB Minni. Flop 5s Ts Qd gives me bottom pair (5s) with a live 4 kicker, plus backdoor straight and flush potential. Board is coordinated with spade flush draw and straight possibilities (e.g., J9, 89), but I have range advantage as preflop raiser. Minni's check suggests weakness or trapping, but c-bet is standard to deny equity to draws and take down pot frequently (~60-70% vs calling range). Her range includes overcards, missed broadways, weak pairs, draws. Half-pot sizing protects my hand, charges draws, maintains fold equity while keeping pot manageable deepstacked. Checking back risks giving free cards to overcards/draws and loses value from worse hands. Bet to continue aggression and realize equity.

Heads-up vs a standard 3bb button open. I have Jc7c — no pair on Q–T–5, only a weak backdoor club possibility and a jack that can pick up a pair. Elon’s small 300 c-bet into the current pot is roughly 1/3 pot, giving me about 25% break-even equity (300/(900+300)). Against a wide heads-up continuation range and with deep stacks, calling is justified to realize equity and pursue implied odds. Raising OOP with this marginal holding would bloat the pot and be too thin; folding would be too nitpicky versus a likely wide stab. Plan: call the 300, keep the pot controlled, and re-evaluate on the turn — continue aggressively only if I pair a jack, pick up meaningful club/straight equity, or get a favorable action; otherwise fold to strong turn raises.

I’m out of position with J♣7♣ on Q♦ T♠ 5♠ K♥. I have no pair and the turn K kills any backdoor club possibility (there are no clubs on board), so my only realistic improvement is pairing a Jack on the river (3 outs, ~6–7% equity). Elon’s line (preflop open, small flop c-bet) is wide but still contains a lot of hands that currently beat me (Qx, Tx, Kx, better overcards, sets). Betting OOP here to fold out middling hands would be too thin and just bloats the pot when I have very little equity. I’ll check to control the pot size, see what Elon does on the river, and only invest more if I actually improve or face a very small river bet I can call as a bluff-catcher; otherwise I’ll fold to sizeable aggression.

Heads-up on button/SB with bottom pair and weak kicker (5s paired with 4c5c on 5sTsQdKh board). Flop c-bet was standard for range advantage and protection, check-call by Minni indicates medium strength: likely draws (spade FD, J9, 89), weak pairs (66-99, Qx/Tx), or Ax. Turn Kh improves some of her Ax hands to top pair and brings broadway straight draws online (JQ, JT). Her second check suggests no top pair/top kicker or monster, but my hand is vulnerable to any K, Q, T, J, or better 5. Pot is 1200, deep effective stacks (~14k). Betting risks getting raised/folded out by better or called by equity; fold equity low after her flop call. Checking behind realizes equity safely (showdown value vs bluffs/draws), avoids bloating pot OOP with marginal hand, controls pot size for river. Prefers pot control over thin value/bet since worse hands fold and better call/raise.

I have J♣7♣ on Q♦ T♠ 5♠ K♥ 8♣ — I missed completely and have no showdown value or equity (no clubs on board, river is live but I didn’t improve). Elon opened wide and c‑bet the flop, then checked both turn and river, a line consistent with either a medium pair or a controlled pot with a decent hand. Bluffing here would be very thin: I have poor blockers and low fold equity, so risking chips OOP with zero real equity is a bad long‑term play. Pot is only 1,200; best play is to check to showdown and preserve my stack. If Elon bets, I’ll fold; since there’s no bet, I’ll check.

River decision heads-up IP with bottom pair poor kicker (5s) on 5s-Ts-Qd-Kh-8c board. Pot 1200, deep effective stacks. Opponent checked OOP all postflop streets after calling flop c-bet. Her range: Qx/Tx/draws/Ax that called flop but wary of turn K; checking river caps her somewhat (unlikely nuts/TPTK). No straight possible, spades missed. My 5s beats bluffs/missed draws/weak Ax but loses to any Tx/Qx/Kx/pairs >5/sets. Thin value bet gets called only by better, bluffs risky vs tight player history. Checking realizes equity safely vs her checking range (showdown value > bet/fold), pot control, preserves stack for 80+ hands left. Consistent with turn checkback plan for marginal hand.

Heads-up on the button with 8♣6♦. Pot is 150 and I only need to call 50, but limp/call is too passive in heads-up; raising takes the initiative and wins many pots preflop. 8‑6o is playable in position — good enough to open a wide button range. I’ll size to 300 (≈3×BB), which is a standard, table-consistent raise size: it applies pressure, gains fold equity, and keeps postflop playability without overcommitting. Plan: fold to a large 3‑bet, call smaller 3‑bets cautiously and use position to c‑bet favorable flops, control the pot on wet boards and avoid bloating pots with marginal holdings.

Heads-up against Minni on the button, who raises frequently from a wide range based on hand history. My K4o is offsuit but has a strong king blocker, blocking top of her range like AK, KQ. Excellent pot odds to call 200 into 400 (2:1 direct, plus implied). Equity around 38-40% vs her loose range. Playable post-flop OOP, can fold to c-bet if miss, or proceed with top pair. Folding too tight, 3-bet not ideal without better blockers or suited connectors. Call to realize equity and control pot size.

Heads-up on a draw-heavy flop (8d9d7h) with two diamonds and open-enders possible. Minni's button raise range is extremely wide. My Kd4s is pure king-high with a diamond blocker, no pair, no straight draw (gutshot unlikely), no flush draw. Minimal equity (~20-30% vs her range, less vs value). OOP first to act, no reason to bet bluff (board favors her range more, she'd continue). Check to realize fold equity or get a free turn if she checks air. Pot odds irrelevant now. Plan: check-fold most bets unless tiny; unlikely to improve profitably.

I have 8♣6♦ on 8♦-9♦-7♥ — top pair (eights) with a weak kicker on a very coordinated, two-diamond flop. Heads-up and in position after opening preflop, I have a range advantage and Elon’s check looks like many hands I can get value from (overcards, weaker pairs, floaters) as well as some missed hands I can fold out. The board is wet (straight and diamond possibilities), so I don’t want to bloat the pot with a marginal top pair. I’ll take the initiative and c-bet a controlled size to extract value and price out some draws while leaving room to fold to a large check-raise. Bet 300 (≈½ pot). If he calls I’ll re-evaluate on the turn; if he check-raises big I will likely fold and preserve chips.

Heads-up OOP on a draw-heavy flop (8d9d7h) with K♦4♠: pure king-high, diamond blocker reduces some of villain's flush draw combos (e.g., KQd, ATd), but no pair, no straight draw (4 gives useless gutshot to 56, unlikely), no flush draw. Villain (Minni) wide button open pre, continues ~50% pot bet, representing wide range including T-x OESD, diamond flush draws, 10-J str8 draws, pairs (88,99,77, but board paired unlikely), and bluffs/air. My equity ~25-35% vs her betting range (blockers help slightly vs value), but precisely: calling 300 into 900 (3:1 odds) requires ~25% equity to break even direct, but OOP multi-street playability poor—king-high rarely good on turn/river, vulnerable to any improvement by villain, reverse implied odds likely. Raising bluff unprofitable (no fold equity vs aggressive villain who pots frequently per history; she'd continue wide/draws/value). Check-fold per plan: this isn't tiny bet (1/2 pot standard), fold to realize no equity disadvantage. Preserve stack for better spots; villain history shows loose play but this board hits her range well.

Heads-up play from the SB (Button) requires a very wide opening range to apply pressure and win blinds frequently. 3h4h is a suited connector with good playability, potential for straights/flushes, and solid implied odds if we hit. Minni has defended the BB loosely in prior hands, calling raises and limping pots often, so a standard raise to 3x the BB builds the pot while maintaining fold equity. Raising to 300 takes initiative, and post-flop we have position on the BB. Folding is too tight, limping invites a raise without initiative. Stacks are deep enough (effective ~144BB) to realize equity.

Facing an open to 300 from the button with K♠T♥ in the big blind. Heads-up KTo is well within a defend range, and calling keeps the pot manageable while we’re out of position. A light 3-bet here would commit more OOP with a marginal hand and could induce 4-bets or larger pots we don’t want to play without a stronger holding. Pot odds to call 200 into a 400 pot (~33%) are reasonable versus the opener’s wide button range. Plan: see the flop, continue aggressively when we hit a King or favorable draw, otherwise proceed cautiously and fold to strong action. Call (total commitment 300).

I have T♣? (T♥) with K kicker — top pair with a strong kicker in a HU spot. Flop Tc 8c 2c is wet-ish because of the two clubs; villain checked, which can indicate weakness or a delayed bluff/slowplay. Betting charges club draws, extracts value from worse tens and small pairs, and denies free equity to many of villain’s speculative hands. I’ll bet ~2/3 pot (400 chips) to protect and build the pot; if he check-raises big I’ll give him credit for a set/two-pair or a made club and fold to heavy aggression, but if he calls I’ll re-evaluate on the turn and continue for value or protection as appropriate.

Preflop raise with suited connector from button is standard for initiative and playability. Flop 8c2cTd completely misses my 3h4h - no pair, no flush draw, no straight draw (not even gutshot; closest is runner-runner wheel or broadway nonsense). Minni's donk bet of 400 into 600 pot reps some strength like pair (88, TT, 82s, etc.), club draw, or straight draw (JQ, KJ). Calling commits 400 into growing pot with zero equity and out of position postflop. Raising as bluff risky: she might have hit and stacks up, low fold equity on coordinated board with her leading. Deep tournament, fold trash to wait for equity spots. Preserve stack for 78 more hands.

Heads-up on the button (I’m the SB) with Q♠3♣ — a hand that’s marginal heads-up but has a queen blocker and good postflop playability in position. Standard strategy is to open-raise wide from the button to seize the initiative and pick up the BB. I’ll raise to 2xBB (200 total) — small, constructive sizing given deep stacks. If Elon 3-bets large I’ll fold (hand is often dominated); if he calls I’ll c-bet favorable, continuation-turn with appropriate sizing and control the pot on wet boards. Raising maximizes fold equity and lets me play the hand in position.

Heads-up preflop from the big blind facing a button minraise to 200 (2x). My 4s3s is a suited connector/gapper with excellent playability, potential for straights (wheel, 3-4-5-6-7, 2-3-4-5-6, etc.), flush draws, and two overcards in a wide sense. Pot odds are excellent: calling 100 more into a 300 pot (3:1 direct, plus implied odds with deep effective stacks ~14k for her). Button ranges are very wide, especially after posting SB. Folding is too tight heads-up; 3-betting risks playing OOP with a speculative hand and bloating the pot; flat calling allows set-mining implied odds and position realization postflop if I hit. Minni is aggressive from history (frequent raises, continuation bets), but calls light too, so value if I connect. Standard defend OOP wide vs button opens.

Flop is Qc Jh 8h, a coordinated board with broadway potential and a heart flush draw possibility. My 4s3s has zero made hand equity: no pair, no straight draw (neither open-ended nor gutshot connects with board), no flush draw (no spades on board, only backdoor). Pure air with minimal backdoor potential. OOP vs aggressive button who minraised pre (wide range: any two, suited connectors, broadway, Ax, etc.). Pot 400, checking to induce bluffs or check-backs from her weak hands, but ready to fold to any reasonable bet size as equity is dismal (~10-15% vs her range at best, worse vs value). Leading out would be a donk bet with nothing, spewy and gives up position. No fold equity worth 3-betting preflop realization. Standard check-fold unless tiny bet, but expect c-bet.

Heads-up in position with Q♠3♣ on 8♥ J♥ Q♣ — I have top pair but a very weak kicker and the board has two hearts (draws) and some backdoor straight potential. Elon checked, which can be both weakness or a disguised strong hand. I should bet for value and protection: a medium-sized bet charges heart draws, gets called by worse queens, jacks and medium pairs, and preserves fold equity. I’ll make a bet of ~60% pot (250). If Elon folds I pick up the pot; if he calls I’ll exercise pot control on the turn and re-evaluate if a heart or overcard appears. If he raises large, I will strongly consider folding given my poor kicker and vulnerability.

Heads-up OOP on QcJh8h flop after defending BB vs button minraise. 4s3s has zero equity: no pair, no straight draw (3-4 misses Q-J-8 entirely), no flush draw (wrong suit), only weak backdoors. Vs Minni's wide button range (c-bets 60-70% flops from history of aggression), villain likely cbets value (QJ, sets, two-pair, strong draws like KTs, 9T suited, flush draws) and bluffs (Ax, random broadways, missed suited connectors). Pot odds ~2.6:1 (250 to call into 650), but realized equity <20% OOP with no semi-bluff raise potential or implied odds on pure air. Calling bloats pot OOP with reverse implied risk if villain slows down; raising is a blatant bluff without fold equity vs calling station tendencies seen in past (e.g., Hand 56 calls river). Check-fold exploits position disadvantage and poor playability, preserving stack for better spots. Consistent with prior flop thought: 'Standard check-fold unless tiny bet'.

Heads-up pre-flop from the button/SB with 5c6c suited connectors. This is a strong speculative hand in position with deep effective stacks (15k). Standard play is to raise to isolate, build the pot for implied odds, or take down the blinds. Raising to 3x the BB (300 total) is a good size given Minni's tendencies to defend but fold to pressure often. Folding or limping is suboptimal as it misses value and position.

Call 300. Heads-up vs the button opening to 3x, T6s is worth defending: I only have to put in 200 more (total 300) to see a flop — pot odds require ~33% equity (200/(400+200)=33%). Versus a typical wide BTN opening range in heads-up, T6s has reasonable equity and very good postflop playability (backdoor spade/straight possibilities and chance to make top pair). I’m out of position postflop, so I don’t want to 3-bet light and build a big pot OOP with this marginal hand; calling keeps the pot manageable, realizes equity, and preserves stack depth for postflop maneuvering. Plan: continue when I connect (top pair, good draws); cede/defend pot control on dry boards against heavy aggression.

Flop is highly coordinated: 3d-5d-4d (three diamonds). I hold T6s with no diamonds — no flush backdoors and only 2/7 as clean straight outs (about 8 raw outs, ~31% to hit by the river). I defended preflop because of heads-up dynamics and the wide opening range, but I don’t want to bloat the pot out of position on a monotone board where I’m unlikely to have the best hand. Checking controls the pot, keeps opponent’s weaker hands and bluffs in the field, and lets me fold to heavy aggression. Plan: check and evaluate Elon’s c-bet — call small bets for price (but fold to large/committing bets); if I hit a 2 or 7 (made straight) or otherwise improve on the turn I will extract value/raise appropriately.

Flop is 3d-5d-4d, giving me the nut straight (3-4-5-6) with 5c-6c. This is very strong, ahead of any pair, two-pair, or lower straight (like A-2 for wheel). Vulnerable mainly to diamond flush or runner-runner higher straight/flush. Minni checked after calling pre-flop from BB, suggesting she has some draw or weak pair but not trapping with monster likely given her history of folding to aggression (e.g., Hands 2,5,14,16). Pot is 600, heads-up deep stacks. Bet pot-sized for value against any piece/draw and to protect against flush draw. Charges max and builds pot with strong hand. Smaller bet risks free card to flush; check risks giving up value.

Heads-up OOP with T6s on 3d-5d-4d. I have no diamonds and no pair — my only realistic outs are 2s and 7s (8 outs), ~31% to make a straight by the river. Elon’s bet is 600 into a 1200 pot; break-even equity to call is 600/(1200+600)=33.3%, so pure draw odds are slightly negative. However the bet is small relative to my 14.7k stack (~4%) and in heads-up the BTN will c-bet a wide range (including many bluffs/missed overcards and single-diamond hands). Calling keeps the pot manageable, realizes my equity, and allows me to fold to heavy turn aggression if I miss. I will not bluff-raise OOP with no showdown value. Plan: call to see the turn; fold to large turn barrels unless I complete the straight or pick up additional equity, and extract value/raise only if I improve.

Check. I’m out of position with T6s on 3d-5d-4d-8c and I have no diamonds and no pair — only 2s and 7s are live (8 outs), ~8/46 ≈ 17.4% to hit the river. The pot is 1,800 and there’s no bet to call on the turn, so betting here would be a pure OOP bluff with little fold equity and would risk bloating the pot with very little equity. Elon has been c-betting a wide range; checking controls the pot, keeps worse hands and bluffs in, and preserves stack for a possible river decision if I hit. Plan: check and fold to a large river bet if I miss; if I hit a 2 or 7 on the river extract value; consider a river bluff only if the board and opponent’s tendencies warrant it.

I hold the nut straight (5-6 on 3d-5d-4d-8c board), the strongest possible hand here. The board is draw-heavy with a completed diamond flush draw and possible straight draws or pairs. Minni has checked pre-flop call and flop bet, checking turn again, suggesting a draw (flush, straight), weak pair, or bluff catcher, but unlikely a monster given her history of calling then folding to continued aggression (e.g., Hands 2, 9, 14). Pot is 1800, her stack 14k effective. Betting pot (1800) builds the pot for value from any calling range (draws, weaker made hands like two-pair or top pair), denies equity to draws (clubs or runner-runner), and maintains pressure. Checking risks giving a free card to improve draws or induces bluffs unnecessarily with nuts. Overbetting risks foldout but pot bet is standard value here heads-up deep.

Fold. Elon just pot-bet the turn for 1,800 into the 1,800 pot (pot is 3,600 after his bet); calling 1,800 now requires roughly 33% equity (1,800 / (3,600+1,800) ≈ 33.3%). With T6s on 3d-5d-4d-8c my only clean outs to the nut straight are 2s and 7s (8 outs → ~17.4% on the river). Including pair-up outs (three remaining 6s and three remaining 10s) raises raw improvement to ~30% but many of those cards still lose to common villain holdings here (diamonds, sets, two-pair) given his flop c-bet and now a large, polarizing turn bet. A raise (min to 3,600) would be a huge, ill-advised OOP bluff with virtually no showdown value. Preserving stack and folding is the +EV play: I’m priced poorly to chase a thin draw into a pot-sized turn bet, so I lay it down and wait for a better spot.

Heads-up on the button (I’m the SB) so open-raising a wide range is standard. T♥4♥ is marginal but has playability (backdoor heart flush, occasional top-pair tens) and performs well in position. By raising to the minimum 200 total (i.e. putting in an extra 150 chips) I seize initiative, create fold equity against the BB’s wide defending range, and keep the pot manageable given our stacks. If Elon folds I scoop the 150-chip pot; if he calls we play the flop in position and proceed based on texture (bet for value/bluffs on favorable boards, check strong/connected boards). If he 3-bets large I’ll heavily favor folding this holding; vs a small 3-bet I may call and play postflop.

95o is a complete trash hand with no playability, straight potential, or suitedness. Facing a button/SB raise to 200 (100 to call into 300 pot), pot odds are about 3:1 but my hand has zero equity against any reasonable range. Defending would be spewy and -EV long-term, especially with deep stacks (my 25k, her 13k). Standard fold to preserve stack for better spots. History shows Minni opens wide from button but this doesn't change the math on this hand.

Heads-up on the button (SB) with A7o, a premium hand in this spot. Wide raising range from SB vs BB in heads-up. Minni has defended wide historically, but position is excellent post-flop. Raise to 3x BB (300 total) to build pot or take it down pre-flop. Pot already 150 with blinds, committing 300 total gives good fold equity and position.

I’m in the big blind with 2♠3♥ facing a standard button open to 300. Calling costs 200 into a 400 pot (pot odds ~25%), but 2-3 offsuit is a very weak, often-dominated hand with terrible postflop playability out of position against a wide but still largely dominating opening range. With deep stacks and the opponent having position, continuing here invites difficult, losing postflop decisions or being exploited by pressure — and a light 3-bet is too thin. Fold and conserve stack for better spots.

Heads-up on the button (I’m the small blind) with QhTs — a very playable, high-equity hand in heads-up. I have ~140BB effective, pot is small (150) and calling 50 is cheap, but limp/calling is too passive: raising takes the initiative, builds value when I'm ahead and generates fold equity. A standard 3x-ish open to 300 is size-consistent with the table and keeps the pot controlled against a deep-stacked, typically aggressive opponent. Plan: if Elon 3-bets large I’ll fold and preserve my deep stack; vs a call or small 3-bet I’ll c-bet favorable Q/T/high-card flops and pot-control on ace-heavy boards. Total commitment: 300.

Heads-up in the big blind facing a standard 3x raise from the small blind/button position. Minni's opening range from SB is very wide (likely 40-60% of hands). K6o is a reasonable defending hand here: king-high with decent playability heads-up, blocks some of her king value hands, and has around 38-42% equity against a typical SB range. Postflop I'll be OOP but can realize equity effectively with position advantage preflop and pot odds (calling 200 into 400 pot for 3:1). Folding would be too tight given the dynamics; 3-betting is possible as a bluff but this hand prefers seeing a flop to realize equity rather than as a bluff. Will call and proceed cautiously postflop based on board texture.

Heads-up pot on a flop of 9c-3s-5s. I defended wide preflop OOP with K6o (Ks6h), which has some king-high showdown value and spade blocker against her very wide SB range (40-60%+). Board is low, uncoordinated but with two spades, hitting flush draws and wheel straight draws (e.g., 24,46). No pair, no strong draw (backdoor spade flush draw at best, no straight draw). Equity roughly 35-40% vs her range if she c-bets wide, but drops OOP. Pot 600, acting first: betting as bluff is low EV (misses value, gets called by better); check allows pot control, realizes equity cheaply if checked behind, or fold to polarized bet. Standard check, prepared to fold to most bets except tiny ones. Long-term stack preservation over 150 hands favors discipline.

I opened to 300 preflop with QTs and got a call. Flop 9c 3s 5s gives me two overcards and a strong spade flush draw (T♠ with 3♠ and 5♠ on board) — a very good semi‑bluff heads‑up. Elon checked, so a continuation bet applies pressure to a wide checking/calling range, picks up the pot often, and builds when I have ~35–38% to make the flush by river plus additional outs pairing the Q or T (overall equity vs a calling range is strong). I size ~2/3 pot (400 into 600) to maximize fold equity while leaving room to fold to a large check‑raise; if called I can continue with equity or pot‑control depending on the turn. Bet 400.

Heads-up OOP on a dry 9c-3s-5s flop with K6o (Ks6h), king-high with Ks spade blocker. Preflop defended wide vs Minni's very wide SB range (40-60%+). No pair, no made draw, only backdoor spade flush draw (minimal). Board hits her suited connectors, spades, low pairs well. Her 400 bet into 600 (~2/3 pot) is standard c-bet sizing, likely polarized (value: 55+,99,set; bluffs: missed Ax,Kx,QJ, gutshots). Equity ~30-35% vs full range but drops OOP, poor implied odds ahead (capped range), bad reverse implied vs draws/pairs. Calling commits ~40% pot OOP, vulnerable to turn barrels on any paint/spade/overcard. Raising as bluff overplays blocker (not nut flush blocker, blocks AKs but she has many better); fold equity low vs aggressive Minni who often has something. Stack preservation over 150 hands prioritizes discipline: fold marginal king-high, induce her bluffs later with better spots. Standard fold.

Heads-up on the button/SB with J3o, a very weak offsuit hand with poor playability and low equity against a calling range. Minni has been aggressive but our stack is deep. Preflop, from SB, we can raise wide to steal, but this hand is too weak even for heads-up ranges—it's dominated often, hard to play postflop. Folding avoids difficult spots and preserves stack for better opportunities in this long tournament.

Heads-up on the button (also SB) with J♥7♥ — a playable, suited hand with decent postflop playability and blocker value. With the pot 150 and only 50 to call, I want to seize initiative and apply pressure, so I’ll min-raise to 200 total. Raising here widens my stealing range, gives me position and fold equity, and keeps the pot manageable with deep stacks (14410 vs 25440). If Elon 3-bets I’ll re-evaluate based on sizing; if he calls I’ll play postflop in position leveraging my backdoor hearts and J-high potential.

Heads-up in BB facing a small button raise to 200 (likely wide range including many speculative hands). A9o is a strong defending hand with good equity against BTN's opening range (~40-50% hands). Pot odds are excellent: calling 100 more (total 200) into 300 pot for 3:1. Post-flop playability is decent OOP with ace-high blocker; can check-call strong boards or take initiative on favorable flops. 3-betting risks larger pots OOP against potential floats/calls; folding too tight given implied odds and stacks (effective ~14k). Standard defend wide in this spot to realize equity while controlling pot size.

Flop: Tc 3s 9s rainbow-ish with spade flush draw. Holding Ah9d for middle pair top kicker (second pair, ace-high). Strong showdown value OOP vs button's wide range (Ax suited/offsuit, broadway, suited connectors, small pairs). Board favors my hand as villain often has overcards, backdoors, or weaker made hands. Equity ~50-60% vs calling range. Checking realizes equity, controls pot size, induces bluffs/misses from floats, allows check-raise strong hands. Betting risks check-raise from sets/draws (TT/99/sets rare) and folds out worse; overbetting unnecessary deep. Pot 400, stacks deep (my 25k, villain 14k), prefer passive line to multi-street value/exploit aggression. History shows Minni c-bets often but folds to resistance.

I raised preflop to seize initiative and Elon just checked the flop (Tc 3s 9s). This is a relatively dry, low-connectivity board that favors a raiser’s wide heads‑up c‑bet range. My J♥7♥ has only a gutshot to an 8 (4 outs ≈ 8.5% to hit on the turn, ≈16% by the river) plus a small backdoor heart possibility; equity is limited but non‑zero. Given the check, I want to keep pressure and fold out many single overcards and weak pairs while keeping the pot manageable with deep stacks, so I’ll make a standard continuation bet about half the pot. If he folds I win the pot; if called I’ll re‑evaluate on the turn (fire again only with improvement or a very good card/odds), and I’ll respect a large check‑raise since my hand’s showdown equity is small.

Facing a half-pot c-bet on Tc3s9s flop OOP with middle pair top kicker (99 with A kicker). Board has spade flush draw and some straight draws (e.g., J8, T8), but my hand blocks strong Ax and has good showdown equity against Minni's wide button range (overcards, weak Ax, suited broadways, small pairs). C-bet is small (200 into 400), consistent with Minni's aggressive history of frequent c-bets but folding to resistance. Pot odds excellent (200 to win 600, ~3.5:1 implied), equity ~45-55% vs polarized range. Calling realizes equity, induces bluffs on turn/river, controls pot size OOP while keeping villain's floats in. Raising risks bloating pot OOP vs draws/sets; folding surrenders too much equity deep-stacked (my 25k vs villain's 14k). Plan: call, evaluate turn (check dry cards, check-raise monsters or strong improvements). Exploits Minni's tendency to fire multiple barrels lightly.

Turn As is an excellent card, pairing my Ace for top pair while already holding the board's Nine, giving me two pair (Aces and Nines) – a very strong hand that crushes most of villain's range. Board texture is draw-heavy with a spade flush draw (3s, 9s, As) and potential straight draws (e.g., J8, T8 for broadways). As OOP first to act in a 800-chip pot with deep effective stacks (~14k villain), betting risks folding out worse hands like weaker Ax, overpairs, or bluffs, while check-raising maximizes value against Minni's aggressive history of frequent continuation bets and multi-street barrels (seen in prior hands like #56, #74). Checking disguises hand strength, induces bets from draws (spade flushes incomplete but scary), Ax highs (AK, AQ without 9 blocker), sets (33/99/TT possible but narrow), and floats. Equity >70% vs villain's flop-calling range post-turn. Plan: check-call thin value or check-raise 2.5-3x bet for value/protection, exploiting Minni's tendency to fire but fold to resistance (e.g., hands #25, #40). Avoids bloating pot prematurely OOP.

Board is Tc 3s 9s As and Elon checked the turn after calling my flop c-bet. I hold J♥7♥ — no pair, no spades, only ~6 clean outs to a single pair and essentially no showdown value vs an Ace or a made spade flush. The Ace on the turn heavily favors Elon's calling range (Ax, T/9 pairs, and now any two-spade combos have a flush), so a turn bet here will often be called by better hands and can get check-raised by the worst of his range that already beats me. With deep stacks and a pot of 800, I want to control the size of the pot and avoid committing chips with very little equity. I’ll check back, see the river, and fold to significant aggression unless I improve or get a very favorable board/small bet to bluff-catch.

River 6d is a complete blank, bricking the spade flush draw and not completing any realistic straight draws (e.g., no help for J8/T8 gutshots). Holding top two pair (Ah-As-9s-9d) OOP first to act in an 800-chip pot vs Minni's button range after check-check turn. This crushes villain's likely holdings: weaker Ax (KQ/AJ/AT w/o 9), 9x (T9/J9 weaker), overpairs/small pairs, missed draws/bluffs. Sets (TT/33 rare, 99 blocked by my 9d) or runner-runner flushes unlikely. Minni's turn check suggests medium strength or missed draw, not a monster (would bet for value/protection). Checking risks free showdown with Ax highs or induces unnecessary calls from worse; betting extracts value from ~40-50% of range that calls (Ax/9x/draws), exploits her history of calling flops/turns lightly but resisting raises (e.g., #25, #40, #74). Size 500 (~60% pot) balances value/thin value while keeping stacks deep (eff. 14k), denies equity to any live draws, and sets up fold equity if she has air. Superior to check as pot odds favor her calling worse; plan pot control if check-raised (unlikely). Maximizes EV long-term.

Elon leads the river for 500 into a pot of 800 (1300 total). Calling 500 to win 1300 requires ~38.5% equity. I hold J♥7♥ — no pair, no spade, effectively no showdown value unless Elon is bluffing. His line (check-call flop to my c‑bet, both check the Ace turn, then a small river bet) is more consistent with a value-heavy range: Ax (now top pair), Tx/9x (pairs or two‑pair), sets, and any two‑spade combos that made a flush — plus a smaller number of missed-draw bluffs. My raw equity versus that range is well below the ~38% threshold (rough estimate ~10–20%), so a call is -EV. A river raise as a bluff is conceivable but too risky here: I have poor blockers, the opponent can easily shove or call with better, and I don’t want to commit a meaningful portion of my stack with essentially zero showdown value. Fold — preserve chips and look for cleaner opportunities to apply pressure.

Heads-up on the button/SB with 87s, a suited connector with excellent playability, straight and flush potential, and good equity against a calling range. Position is ideal for realizing equity post-flop. Minni defends wide from BB based on history. Raising thin value/steal blinds with a standard 3x size (300 total) takes initiative, builds pot when ahead, and applies pressure while keeping good implied odds if called. Folding or limping suboptimal; raise optimal EV play.

I’m in the big blind with 9♠6♠ facing a button open to 300. The pot is 400 and it costs me 200 to call (I’ll be 300 total), so I need ~33% equity to break even. 9♠6♠ is suited and has good playability heads‑up (flush/straight potential) and versus a wide/button opening it has enough equity and implied odds given deep stacks (~140 BB) to defend. I’ll avoid a small 3‑bet to 500 — that sizing is too small to fold him off many hands and could get punished by a bigger 4‑bet. Being OOP postflop I plan to call, see a flop, and continue only with meaningful pairs or strong draws; otherwise use pot control and fold to heavy aggression.

I’m OOP in the big blind with 9♠6♠ on J♠ 2♣ A♣. I have no pair and only a very weak backdoor spade possibility (needing both turn+river spades — ~4%), while I have only modest pair outs (~8) that often still lose to any A or J in Elon’s raising range. Elon’s button open heavily contains Ax/broadways and will c-bet this board a lot, so I want to keep the pot small and avoid getting committed with a weak drawing hand. I’ll check to control the pot and see his action; I’ll fold to a standard c-bet or a larger bet, but can consider calling a very small stab to see a cheap turn. If checked to, I’ll re-evaluate on the turn and only continue with a pair or a clear draw.

Flop Js 2c Ac is an ace-high board with some flush draw potential (clubs and spades), but my 87s gives me a strong spade flush draw (9 outs) plus overcards/live cards and position for excellent playability. Minni checked after calling pre-flop from BB, suggesting a wide defending range including Ax, broadways, pairs, suited connectors – many of which I have equity against. With initiative from my pre-flop raise, this is a prime semi-bluff spot: betting builds the pot when ahead (possible backdoor straights or Ax blockers implied), applies pressure to her weak range, denies equity to her draws/improvers, and pot controls if called. Checking back realizes equity too cheaply and gives free cards to overcards/draws. Standard 2/3 pot bet (400) sizes for value from worse Ax/pairs while protecting the pot. High EV in deep-stack tournament play.

I checked the flop OOP with 9♠6♠ on J♠ 2♣ A♣ and Elon fired 400 into the pot (pot now ~1000), costing me 400 to call. That call requires roughly 28–29% equity to break even. I have no pair, only a very weak backdoor spade (need both turn+river spades, ~4%) and only a small chance to hit a pair by the river; many of those pair-outs still lose to any A or J in his betting range. A button open/CB on this board is weighted toward Ax, Jx and club/overcard hands that dominate me, and his sizing is sizable — not a cheap probe to see a turn. OOP against a likely strong c-betting range, this is a marginal multi-street spot with poor equity. I fold and preserve my deep stack for a better, more profitable spot.

Heads-up on the button (small blind) with Qc6s — a marginal hand but very playable in heads-up where ranges should be wide. I want to take the initiative and use position postflop, so I’ll open-raise rather than just completing. A standard 3x BB open to 300 (total) gives fold equity, narrows Elon’s calling range, and keeps pot sizes manageable; if he 3-bets I’ll fold this hand most of the time, if called I’ll c-bet favorable flops and control the pot on dangerous boards.

Heads-up in BB vs Minni's button/SB raise to 300. My Ks5s is a strong suited king, with good playability, blockers to strong hands, and excellent pot odds (calling 200 into 400 pot, over 2:1). Minni raises wide from SB in late position based on history. Defending wide here maximizes EV long-term. Will call and play post-flop cautiously, leveraging suited potential for straights/flushes/draws. Raising risks too much with speculative hand deep-stacked (effective ~13k). Folding too tight.

Middle pair (fives) with king kicker on a 4-5-8 rainbow board. Decent showdown value, backdoor spade flush draw, and overcard protection with K blocker. OOP vs aggressive button/SB who raises wide pre and c-bets frequently based on history (e.g., Hands 69,71,72,73,77). Checking induces bluffs, allows check-raise value/protection if strong, and controls pot with speculative hand deep-stacked (my 25k covers her 13k). Betting risks fold equity loss on worse pairs/draws and vulnerable to raises from 6-7, 98, sets, overpairs. Pot odds favor seeing turn cheap. Long-term EV maximized by playing cautiously as planned pre-flop.

Heads-up on a very dry flop (5h 8c 4d). I hold Qc6s — no pair, only a weak gutshot to a 7 (4 outs) and no realistic flush possibility. Elon checked so I can take the initiative with a standard heads-up c-bet to fold out lots of his missed preflop calling range (Kx, Tx, Jx, small pairs, air). The pot is 600, so betting 300 (≈50%) is a balanced size: it demands ~33% fold equity to be profitable and keeps the pot controllable given deep stacks. If he calls I’ll proceed cautiously on the turn (check back or continue small on safe runouts); if he raises I’ll fold — Q6 has poor equity vs aggression. Bet 300 to seize initiative and pick up the pot now.

Top pair (fives) with king kicker on dry 4-5-8 rainbow flop. Excellent pot odds calling 300 into 900 (pot becomes 1200, ~4:1). Minni's small 1/3 pot c-bet from button/SB likely wide range including bluffs, worse pairs, draws (6-7, 3-6). OOP call keeps pot controlled, realizes equity against her aggression, preserves stack deep (eff. 13k). Raising risks bloating pot OOP vs potential monsters (sets, 8x); folding too tight given strength/SDV. Backdoor spade draw adds playability. Proceed cautiously to turn as pre-flop plan.

OOP on turn with middle pair top kicker (5s with K) on 4-5-8-T rainbow board. Pot control essential deep-stacked (eff. ~13k, pot 1200 small). Minni's flop c-bet (1/3 pot) wide from button/SB aggression history (e.g., frequent small bets in 69,71,72+ recent hands). Turn Ts improves some draws (spade flush possible, though I block Ks; gutshots like 6-7,9-J straights). Checking induces her continuation bets/bluffs from worse pairs/draws (6x,9x,Tx weak, air), allows cheap showdown or check-raise value/protection vs. strong hands (sets,88, two-pair). Betting risks: thin value from callers, vulnerable to raises OOP from 88/TT/sets/straights/draws; denies her bluff equity. Folding impossible. Long-term EV from pre/flop defense: realize equity cautiously, leverage position by checking.

Board is 5h 8c 4d T with pot 1,200 and SPR ≈11 (stack 13,160). My Qc6s has missed — no pair and only very skinny outs — and Elon’s call on the flop indicates he has at least some equity (a pair, a float, or a ten). Betting the turn would be a pure bluff with limited fold equity and puts me in a tough spot if he raises or calls. With deep stacks I want to control the pot and use position to see the river and pick better spots to bluff or fold. I’ll check back the turn to realize equity, avoid bloating the pot with air, and reassess on the river: if he bets big I’ll fold most of the time; if he checks back a safe river I can consider a small delayed bluff.

River OOP with middle pair top kicker (5s-Ks) on 4-5-8-T-J rainbow board (two spades Ts/Js). Pot 1200 small relative to deep effective stacks (~13k). Board texture draw-heavy: straights (6-7 for 4-5-6-7-8), flush completes (spades), possible two-pairs (8-4/5-T/J-T), sets (44/55/88/TT/JJ), overpairs. My hand has solid SDV vs her wide SB/button range (frequent small c-bets/checks behind recent hands 69-80 show mixed aggression/passivity), blocks K-high pairs/straights. She checked turn after flop c-bet/call, polarizing to value (sets/Tx/Jx strong, 6-7 straight) or weak missed draws (9x, flush chases, air). Betting risks: thin value from worse (4x/8x low, Tx/Jx weak), vulnerable to raises from nuts/straights (no fold equity needed), denies her bluffs. Checking maximizes EV: induces bluffs/missed draws, cheap showdown vs worse pairs/draws, pot control OOP deep-stacked prevents overcommitment. Consistent with pre/flop/turn cautious defense plan realizing equity long-term. No fold, value bet marginal EV due position.

Heads-up, I’ve missed the board with Qc6s (no pair) and the river checked to me. Pot is 1,200 and stacks are deep (I have 13,160; Elon 25,640). Elon’s line (check-call flop, check turn, check river) is a passive one — consistent with weak pairs, small pocket pairs, overcards that floated the flop, or air. Checking through the turn preserved pot control; on the river a small-to-medium bluff can often pick up the pot against his checked range. I want to apply pressure without overcommitting: a half-pot bet (600) forces him to have ~33% or better equity to call (600/(1200+600)=33%), which is higher than the equity most pure floats/air have. If he has a small pair (5s/8s/4s) he may still call, but many overcards and missed hands should fold. If he raises I’ll fold — my hand has essentially no showdown value. If he calls I’ll concede the pot and adjust opponent read for future spots. Betting 600 maximizes my chance to steal this pot while keeping the loss limited if called.

River OOP facing 600 bet into 1200 pot (~4:1 odds, need ~20% equity). Middle pair top kicker (5s-Ks) with spade blocker on draw-heavy 4-5-8-T-J board (straights 6-7, spade flush poss., pairs/sets). Minni's turn check then river bet polarizes to value (sets 44/55/88/TT/JJ, 6-7 straight, Jx/Tx strong) or bluffs (missed spades 9x/AKs/Qs, gutshots). Her SB/button aggression history (frequent small bets 69-80, but checks behind) includes bluffs/value mix; turn check weakens overpairs/Tx. My hand crushes bluffs/worse (4x, weak Tx/Jx, missed draws), blocks Ks flushes/straights. SDV sufficient vs polarized range; pot odds mandate call over fold (too tight OOP after pot control). Raise risks bloating vs monsters OOP deep eff. 12.5k, low fold equity. Consistent pre/flop/turn cautious play: realize equity, induce/value control. Call extracts max EV long-term.

94o is a very weak offsuit hand with poor playability, low equity, and no high card strength. In the SB vs BB heads-up, while ranges widen, this hand is still a clear fold preflop to avoid difficult post-flop spots and spew chips. Pot odds to call 50 into 150 are decent but don't justify playing trash. Preserve stack for better spots in this late tournament stage with deep stacks.

Heads-up on the button (I’m the SB) with 5♠3♦ — a weak, unconnected offsuit hand but I have position. The pot is 150 and it costs me 50 to see the flop, so calling is very cheap (I’m getting ~3:1 pot odds). Raising to 200 would commit extra chips with a marginal hand and likely just get called by a wide BB range or shoved on by a stronger hand; folding here is too tight in heads-up. Plan: call to see the flop and use positional advantage — proceed cautiously on dry boards (check/fold to large aggression) and be willing to take the pot when I hit a pair or a reasonable draw.

Heads-up pre-flop in the big blind facing a small completion/limp from Minni in the small blind/button. My hand T6s is suited with straight potential and good playability, making it a strong hand to defend wide in this spot. Rather than checking and playing post-flop out of position, I raise to take the initiative, build the pot when ahead, and apply pressure on Minni who has shown a tendency to fold to aggression in previous hands. Raising to 300 (3x the big blind) is a standard sizing for thin value and fold equity.

Heads-up on the button with 5♠3♦ — a weak, unconnected offsuit hand. Elon raised to 300 but the price to see a flop is cheap: calling 200 into a 400 pot gives about 3:1 pot odds and I retain positional advantage. Raising here is unnecessary and would bloat the pot with a marginal hand; folding is too tight given the cheap price and deep stacks.
