Plan: call to see the flop, play straightforward postflop (check/fold to heavy aggression on dry boards, take the pot when I pair or hit a strong draw, apply pressure when appropriate).

Flop of 3s-9h-3c completely misses my Tc6s, leaving me with just ten-high and negligible equity (backdoor spade draw at best). However, this is an ideal continuation bet spot: the board is extremely dry and paired low, heavily capping Minni's limp-call range to weak hands like 3x, small pairs below 9, or suited connectors that rarely improved. Minni has shown passive tendencies and folds frequently to post-flop aggression in the hand history. With position out of position but deep stacks (27k effective), a well-sized c-bet exploits this by generating frequent folds from her overcards, floats, or marginal hands while denying her equity. Pot is 600; betting 2/3 pot (400) balances thin value from any 3x or 9x she might have with high fold equity. If called or raised, I can fold cleanly without much loss. Checking invites a bet or free card to her draws, reducing my fold equity. This aggression aligns with GTO strategy in OOP c-bet spots on low-paired boards.

I flopped trip threes (I have 3♦ and the board is 3♠ 9♥ 3♣) — an extremely strong holding and effectively the nuts versus nearly all of Elon’s range on this flop (only pocket 9s would currently beat me). Elon bet 400 into a 1000 pot, representing a wide range of one-pair hands and bluffs. I want to extract value and charge any potential floats/draws, so I should raise rather than just calling and giving a cheap turn card. I’ll raise to 2000 total on the flop — large enough to get calls from worse pairs (9x, Kx, Qx, medium pairs) and some pocket pairs, but not so huge as to unnecessarily overcommit right now. If Elon jams over this I’ll seriously consider calling because trips is very strong here.

Facing a large raise to 2000 (1600 more to call) into a 3000 pot on a dry paired flop (3s-9h-3c) where my Tc6s has zero equity—no pair, no draws beyond a weak backdoor spade. My pre-flop raise and flop c-bet was a standard semi-bluff exploiting Minni's passive limp-call range, but her sizable raise polarizes her range to strong hands like 33, 99, 3x (A3s/K3s/Q3s), or maybe sets/overpairs she trapped with. Weaker hands like floats, overcards, or unpaired Ax don't raise this big OOP to my c-bet. Pot odds are ~2.8:1, but with <10% equity against her value-heavy raising range, calling commits too much of my stack (1600/26690 ~6%) for no upside. Shoving or raising as a bluff is disastrous against her committed sizing (nearly 20% of her 10k stack) and likely cooler range. Folding is the disciplined play, preserving stack for better spots in this heads-up dynamic where Minni has shown strength when raising post-flop.

Heads-up on the button/small blind with K♠5♠, a suited king-high hand with good playability, straight/flush potential, and position post-flop. Standard to raise frequently from this spot to take initiative, build the pot, and pressure the BB. Minni has defended wide historically. Raising to 3x the BB (300 total) is a balanced size for value/thin value and fold equity.

I’m in the big blind with 2♠4♣ facing Elon’s button/SB open to 300. This is a very weak, unplayable hand out of position — a small 3‑bet to 500 would be a thin bluff and will often get called, leaving me OOP with poor equity. Pot odds to call are attractive (call 200 more to see a flop into ~600), so I’ll defend by calling and see a flop. Postflop plan: fold to significant aggression unless I pair or pick up strong backdoor draws; otherwise preserve my deep stack for better spots.

I have 2♠4♣ on 2♥ T♦ 8♦ — I flopped a small pair (pair of twos) but I’m out of position vs the button. Elon opened preflop and is likely to continuation‑bet missed overcards and diamond draws. I prefer pot control with this marginal holding: checking keeps the pot small, induces bluffs/draws, and gives me information. Plan: check and call a small‑to‑medium c‑bet to realize my showdown value; fold to heavy aggression or over‑pot shove unless I improve. If Elon checks back I’ll re‑evaluate on the turn and only start building the pot with a clear improvement (two‑pair/trips) or a safe blocker/stack‑dependent line.

Preflop, I raised K♠5♠ from the button as a standard play with position, suited king-high for playability. Flop is 2h Td 8d, a somewhat coordinated board with diamond flush draw and straight possibilities, but my king-high has good blocker value to strong hands like KT, AT, etc., and remains live. Minni checked, giving me initiative in position. With range advantage as preflop raiser, this is a prime spot for a continuation bet to deny equity to her wide defending range, take down the pot frequently, or set up turns. Historical tendencies show Minni folds to postflop aggression often when checked. Betting ~2/3 pot (400 into 600) balances value (occasional top pair/overpair) with bluffs, exploits her passivity, and maintains pressure in a heads-up dynamic to build my stack.

I have 2♠4♣ on 2♥ T♦ 8♦ — a small pocket pair (pair of twos). Elon opened wide from the button and has c‑bet 400 into a ~1000 pot (bet size ~40%); pot odds to call are roughly 28.6%. Heads‑up his continuation range is wide (many overcards, some diamond draws and occasional made pairs), so my pair has reasonable showdown value and good implied odds with deep stacks. I’ll call to keep the pot controllable, realize equity and see the turn; I will fold to substantial turn aggression unless I improve to two‑pair/trips or pick up a clear draw. I won’t raise OOP with this weak holding and no strong blockers.

Board: 2♥ T♦ 8♦ J♦. My hand: 2♠4♣ — bottom pair with no diamond. Elon was the preflop raiser and c‑bet the flop (I called), so his range contains many diamonds, overcards and some made hands. The turn J♦ completes a lot of diamond draws and makes my lone pair extremely vulnerable. With no diamond blocker and deep stacks, bluffing or building the pot here is poor value — I have almost no fold equity and will be behind vs any diamond or most top pairs. I’ll check to keep the pot small and concede to aggression: if Elon bets I’ll fold (only calling an extremely tiny probe that’s off pattern), if he checks we go to showdown and I take my small showdown value. Preserving my stack for better spots is the priority.

Heads-up on turn with K♠5♠, king-high no pair no draw on a 2h-T♦-8♦-J♦ board. Preflop raise and flop cbet were standard light aggression with position pre but acting first postflop. Minni's double check suggests weakness or trapping, but her history shows passivity and frequent folds to continued aggression (e.g., Hands 5,14,16,40,42,71,72,73,77,80). Pot 1400, her stack 12k allows pressure without committing her. Turn J♦ completes flushes and straights (e.g., Q9, 79, any two diamonds), bad for my range but also caps hers if checking strong. King-high has showdown value vs missed draws/bluffs in her wide BB defending range, but betting exploits her passivity: ~60-70% pot bet denies equity, gets folds from worse (e.g., pocket pairs below J, weak T/8), value from floats, fold equity high given villain tendencies. Checking risks giving free card to draws or induces river bluffs but misses dead money. With tournament goal to build stack, continue pressure in favorable dynamic; bet 1000 (2/3 pot) balances range.

I have bottom pair (2♠4♣) on 2♥ T♦ 8♦ J♦ and no diamond blocker. Elon opened BTN, c‑bet the flop and now bets 1,000 into a 2,400 pot. Calling 1,000 requires ~29% equity, but my hand has very little real equity — only two outs to trips (one is a diamond) and I lose to any diamond, Jx, Tx or better pair. Raising OOP with bottom pair is poor strategy and I have almost no fold equity if I call. With deep stacks and plenty of chips to wait for better spots, folding and preserving my stack is the correct play.

Heads-up on the button (SB) with Th2d: it's a weak hand but in heads-up I should be opening a very wide range to seize initiative and use positional advantage. Calling 50 is cheap, but raising gains fold equity and lets me take control of the pot; a standard 3x-BB open keeps the sizing in-line with the table and reduces the frequency of light 3-bets. Plan: raise to 300 to apply pressure; fold to a large/committed 3-bet, but if called use position to continuation-bet favorable flops and control the pot size with marginal holdings.

Js4c is a speculative trash hand with minimal equity against a button raise from Minni, who has been aggressive in recent hands. Out of position in the BB, folding preserves my deep stack (27k) for better spots in this heads-up phase with 85/150 hands played. No reason to call 200 into 400 pot with this unplayable hand.

Heads-up on the button/SB with 83 suited. Wide opening range from this position. Hand has suited connector potential for straights/flushes postflop, plus fold equity against BB. Standard to raise small to steal blinds or build pot in position. Raising to 300 (3x BB) is balanced size given deep stacks and history of aggression from opponent.

Elon opened to 300 from the button in a heads-up spot. I have K♦6♠ — a marginal but defendable hand heads-up. The pot is 400 and calling 200 more (300 total committed) requires ~33% equity to break even; versus a wide button opening range K6o usually has at least that. Stacks are deep, and I don’t want to bloat the pot out of position with a light 3-bet, so I’ll defend and see a flop. Postflop I’ll proceed cautiously OOP: continue with top pair or strong draws, fold to heavy aggression on dry boards or when clearly dominated.

Check. I defended preflop with K♦6♠ heads-up but on A♥–5♦–8♠ I have no pair and no realistic draw (only a very remote backdoor spade). Out of position I don’t want to bloat the pot with a thin bluff; Elon’s opening range contains many A‑x and better hands that will continue, and my equity vs that range is poor. Checking controls the pot and lets me fold to clear strength while still allowing me to call a small probe to see a cheap turn. Plan: if Elon checks back I re-evaluate the turn; if he c-bets small (≈≤200, ~1/3 pot) I may call to realize equity, but I’ll fold to medium/large bets (>200) unless I improve (e.g., pair a king).

Middle pair (eights) with a weak 3d kicker on an ace-high flop. The board is Ah-8s-5d, which brings top pair danger, but Minni's check suggests possible weakness—no ace or strong hand. As the preflop aggressor in position (acting last postflop in heads-up), a continuation bet is standard to protect my hand, charge draws (like 6-7 straight draw), and extract value from weaker pairs (fives), overcards, or bluffs. My range hits this board decently, and opponent calls preflop wide from BB. Sizing 400 (about 2/3 pot) balances value and fold equity without overcommitting. Checking risks giving free cards to improve past me. Deep stacks allow for postflop playability.

Elon opened the button and continuation‑bet 400 into a 1000 pot on A♥‑5♦‑8♠. I have K♦6♠ — no pair and no realistic draw (only a remote backdoor spade). Calling 400 to win a 1400 pot requires ~28.6% equity; my chance to hit a king by the river is only ~12.5% and even if I pair a king I can still be behind many of Elon’s continuing hands (lots of A‑x and other made hands in a button raising/c‑betting range). I’m out of position and don’t want to bloat the pot with a marginal hand, so I’ll fold and wait for a better spot or position to apply pressure.

Heads-up on the button with J♦4♣ — a weak, but playable hand in position. The pot is 150 and it costs me 50 to see a flop (min-raise to 200). With position and deep stacks, calling is the best option: the price is cheap (50 into a 200 pot after my call — ~25% required equity) and I can use post‑flop position to realize equity or fold to heavy pressure. A min‑raise to 200 would commit more chips with a marginal holding and likely get action from stronger hands Elon defends with, so I prefer to keep the pot small and see a flop. Plan: call, evaluate the flop for a pair/draw or use position to pressure when appropriate.

Heads-up against Minni in the SB who limped (completed SB to full BB). Pot is 200. My hand Ah7h is a strong suited ace heads-up: good high card value, suited for flush potential, live cards, and excellent playability. SB limp range is extremely wide, including many dominated hands and trash. Raising for value, to take initiative OOP, build the pot when likely ahead, and charge draws. Standard raise size around 3-4BB total (300-400), choosing 400 to apply good pressure without overcommitting. Deep stacks allow postflop flexibility. Checking would allow free flops with position disadvantage.

Heads-up on the button with J♦4♣. Elon raised to 400 after I completed to 100 (pot is 500, I must put in 300 more). Calling 300 into a 500 pot makes the total pot 800, so I need ~37.5% equity to break even. My hand is weak but playable in position and Elon has opened fairly widely heads-up in previous hands, so I expect a reasonably wide raising range. I’ll call to use my positional advantage: I can fold to heavy aggression if I miss or play for value/bluffs when I connect. I won’t 3-bet with J4o (that would be too speculative and inflate the pot out of position postflop if called), and folding would be too passive given heads‑up dynamics and stack depth. Plan: call to see a flop, continue only with a pair, strong draw, or favourable board texture; otherwise concede to pressure.

Top pair top kicker (7h pairs 7s, Ah kicker) on a coordinated, draw-heavy flop (7s 3d 6s) with open-ended straight draws (4-5) and nut flush draw (spades). OOP heads-up vs SB limper-caller, whose range is wide: Ax, smaller pairs, suited connectors, weak broadways. Strong value hand that wants protection from draws and to build pot when ahead vs worse (e.g., 65, 76, A-high). Checking risks giving free cards to equity-heavy hands. Bet 1/2 pot (400) balances value, protection, and fold equity while keeping SPR reasonable (~13:1 post-bet). Deep stacks but her shorter stack (12k) limits overplay.

Elon has bet 400 into a 1200 pot — calling 400 requires roughly 25% equity. J♦4♣ has almost no showdown value now (only ~24% to pair by river), but heads‑up and in position his flop c‑bet range is wide. Folding is too passive for a relatively small price; a bluff‑raise is thin because he calls/3‑bets a lot and I have poor equity if called. I’ll call to use my position and float — if he checks the turn I can take the pot with a bluff, if he bets small I can call again, and if he barrels very large I’ll fold unless I’ve improved. If I pair a J or 4 on the turn I’ll shift to value‑oriented betting/raising.

On the turn, board is 7s 3d 6s 4d. I have top pair top kicker (7h pairing the 7s with Ah kicker), which is still very strong heads-up. The board is draw-heavy: persistent spade flush draw, and the 4d completes straights for hands like 5x (3-4-5-6-7 straight). Possible opponent range from SB limp-call pre and flop call: weak Ax (dominated), smaller pairs (55,44 now vulnerable), 6x, 5x draws that hit or semi-bluff, suited spades. OOP, betting continues to protect against equity from remaining draws (spades ~19% equity if calling), deny equity to floats/A-high, and extract value from worse made hands (weaker kickers, 6x, 86 etc.). Checking risks giving free river cards to draws or allowing her to bluff/value bet thinner in position. Pot SPR ~7:1 post a 2/3 pot bet, manageable deep. Sizing 1100 (2/3 pot) balances value/protection/fold equity without overbetting vs her 11k stack. Her history shows calling stations tendencies, so value while ahead.

I just paired my 4 on the turn (J♦4♣ => pair of 4s). Elon has shown a fairly wide, aggressive line (raise pre, c‑bet flop, barrel turn), so his range contains both value (7x/6x/3x, sets, some overpairs) and bluffs/missed overcards. Pot is 2700 and it costs me 1100 to call (~29% break‑even equity). In heads‑up play with position a single pair like this is worth continuing: calling keeps worse hands and bluffs in, preserves pot control, and lets me use position on the river. Raising here would be thin (it polarizes me and builds a big pot against sets/two‑pair), while folding would be too passive given the price. Plan: call 1100, see the river, and re‑evaluate — if he checks I can bet thin for value, if he bets a small/medium amount I’ll often call, and if he jams an overbet on a scary river I’ll fold unless I’ve improved significantly.

River 2c is a relatively blank card that doesn't complete the spade or diamond flushes and only marginally helps some straight draws (no new straights completed without a 5). I hold top pair top kicker (7h-Ah), which remains very strong heads-up against Minni's SB limp-call pre, flop call on draw-heavy board, and turn call. Her range is capped somewhat: possible straights (5x for 3-4-5-6-7), sets (unlikely given action), two pairs (e.g., 76, 64), but includes many worse hands like weaker pairs (6x, 3x), Ax high, busted draws, and worse made hands (87, mid pairs). History shows Minni as a calling station who frequently calls bets with marginal holdings (e.g., Hands 15,37,45,56). Checking risks her checking behind worse or thin value betting; betting extracts value from her wide calling range while protecting against bluffs. Pot 3800, bet 2400 (~2/3 pot) sizes for value, keeps her pot odds poor, and leaves her ~8k behind for uncomfortable calls. Deep effective stacks (hers 10.5k) support continued aggression OOP to maximize EV.

River decision: I have J♦4♣ — a small pair (4s) on 7♠ 3♦ 6♠ 4♦ 2♣. Pot is 6,200 and it costs 2,400 to call (~28% break‑even). Elon opened pre, bet the flop, bet the turn and now bets the river (a consistent 3‑street value/aggressive line). That line is heavily weighted toward hands that beat a single small pair — sets (7s/6s/3s/possible 4s), two‑pair combos (e.g. 7+6) and top‑pair hands (7x/6x) — and those hands will continue this line very frequently. I only beat rare weaker pairs or pure bluffs. To justify a call I need ~28% equity, but against the likely value‑heavy range my equity with second pair is well below that.

Raising as a bluff is thin — I block very few of the villain’s value combos, I have poor fold equity facing a player who has fired three streets, and if called I’m crushed. Preserving chips in this spot and waiting for a better spot heads‑up is the +EV line. Therefore I fold.

Heads-up pre-flop from the SB (Button) vs BB. Q7s is a strong suited hand with good playability, queen-high blocker, and straight/flush potential. Deep stacks (mine 29k, opponent 10k). Pot is 150 with 50 to call, but limping is weak; better to raise for value/thin value and fold equity. Standard raise size to 3x BB (300 total) to build pot or take it down immediately. Minni has shown folding tendencies in similar spots from history.

I'm in the big blind with As 8h facing a button open to 300 (pot 400; I must call 200). Heads-up A8o is strong enough to defend — calling 200 more (300 total committed) gives ~33% break-even equity (200 into a ~600 pot) and performs well versus a wide button opening range. I avoid 3-betting here because I'm out of position and A8o is marginal as a preflop 3-bet; calling keeps the pot controllable and lets me realize equity and play postflop. Plan: see the flop, proceed cautiously OOP, fold to very heavy action on dry boards and look for spots to bluff or extract value on favorable textures.

Flop is very dry (6h-3c-2h). I have As8h: just ace-high, no pair and not a real draw (one heart in hand with two on board is not a flush draw). I’m out of position — betting OOP with only ace-high is suboptimal. Check to control the pot and induce a BTN c-bet. I’ll call small-to-medium cbets (roughly ≤ ~1/2 pot ≈ 300) to realize showdown equity and potentially float; fold to larger, committed sizing. If the turn brings a safe improvement (an A) or a clear bluffing opportunity, I can proceed then.

Heads-up on the button postflop with Qs7s after raising preflop and getting called by Minni in BB. Flop 6h3c2h is low and somewhat drawy with heart flush draw and wheel straight draws possible, but my queen-high has decent showdown value and blocks strong Qx hands. As preflop raiser, my range hits this board better (overpairs, higher pairs, gutshots), while Minni's calling range includes weaker broadways, Ax suited, low connectors that often miss. She checks, indicating weakness; c-bet for thin value against her worse high cards/ace-high and fold equity against draws. Half-pot sizing balances protection vs draws with keeping pot manageable deep vs her shorter stack (~10k). History shows Minni folds frequently to postflop aggression.

Elon c-bet 400 into a 1000 pot — I must call 400. Required equity is ~28.6% (400/(1000+400)). Heads-up my A♠8♥ has decent showdown equity vs a wide button c-bet range and also blocks some Ace combos; the flop is very dry so many of his bets are continuations rather than made hands. Raising OOP with just ace-high and no real draw is a mistake — I want to keep the pot controlled and realize equity, and I can attempt to float and take the pot away on a safe turn or extract value if I hit. Plan: call 400 to see the turn; fold to very large turn aggression if I don’t improve, but if I pair the Ace (or otherwise improve) I will start betting for value.

Turn: board is 6♥-3♣-2♥-2♦ and I hold A♠8♥ — only Ace-high, no real draws and out of position. Elon bet the flop and then checked the paired turn; betting OOP here with just ace-high is a losing line (it bloats the pot, gives him a free check‑raise with hands that picked up a deuce, and I have very little fold equity). I’ll check to control the pot and see the river for free. Plan: if the river pairs me (an A) I’ll look to get value; if I miss and Elon bets big I’ll fold; if he checks again I can consider a small bluff on a suitable river card but otherwise avoid further investment without improvement.

Heads-up on button with Qs7s after preflop raise and flop c-bet called by Minni (BB, shorter stack ~10k). Turn 2d pairs the deuces, low board favors my preflop raising range (overpairs, higher broadways, sets) over her calling range (Ax, suited connectors, low pairs that may have improved weakly). Board drawy but pairing kills some straight draws; hearts still live but my spade FD adds equity. Minni checks again, showing weakness per history of folding to aggression. Barrel for thin value vs her Ax/worse highs/low pairs and max fold equity vs missed draws/improved weak hands. Pot bet ~1400 pressures her stack (70% pot), balances my range, builds pot OOP. Check risks giving free card to draws or letting her realize equity cheaply.

Fold. The pot is 2800 and Elon just bet 1400 on the turn, so I need ~1400/(2800+1400) ≈ 33% equity to make a call correct. I have A♠8♥ on 6♥-3♣-2♥-2♦ — only ace-high and no realistic draw (the turn killed the backdoor heart possibility). Elon opened preflop, c-bet the dry flop and now fires a large turn bet into a paired board; that line is weighted toward value (6x, 3x, hands that improved on the turn, sets/two-pair) and is unlikely to contain enough bluffs to give me the ~33% equity. Out of position, calling here simply puts me in a tough river spot and risks a larger loss; folding preserves my stack for better spots.

I’m on the button (SB) with Ad Tc — a strong heads‑up holding: good Ace blocker and dominates weaker Aces/broadways. Pot is 150 and I only need to call 50 to see a flop, but I want to seize the initiative and build value/fold equity. Elon is very deep and tends to call opens fairly wide, so a standard 3x BB sizing is appropriate: large enough to fold out some hands and get value from worse Aces and broadways, but small enough to keep post‑flop playability with ~97BB effective. If Elon calls I can c‑bet with range advantage; if he 3‑bets I’ll re‑evaluate based on sizing and his tendencies. Therefore I raise to 300 (total committed).

Heads-up situation on hand 89/150 with deep stacks (30k effective vs Minni's 9.5k). In BB facing a button/SB raise to 300 (to call 200 into 400 pot, ~2:1 odds). J9o is a playable hand heads-up with straight potential, overcard blocker, and decent equity vs a wide button range. Minni has been aggressive from late position throughout history, often raising light and c-betting flops but folding to resistance. Defending wide OOP vs button is standard; calling keeps pot multiway potential low (heads-up), realizes equity, and allows post-flop flexibility with implied odds. Folding too tight; 3-betting risks variance with a marginal blocker hand better suited for calling. Plan: Call, see flop, c/c strong flops, check-fold weak ones, check-raise turns if draw hits.

Flop Js6h6d gives me top pair (Jd) with a 9 kicker, a strong hand heads-up OOP on a paired board that reduces some straight draw equity. Minni's button opening range is wide (broadways, suited connectors, Ax, pairs), and she'll c-bet frequently (~60-70% based on history of aggression). Checking realizes equity, induces bluffs/value from worse (AJ, KQ, 66,77-55, draws like 78/57), controls pot size, and sets up check-raise vs overbets or check-call smaller cbets. Donk betting is suboptimal OOP with this hand as it bloats pot and denies her bluffing opportunity. Effective stacks are deep (her 9.5k), so post-flop playability is key. Plan: Check, call most cbets (fold only to large overbet showing strength), raise turn/river if improves or vs weakness.

I’m the preflop aggressor in position with AdTc on a dry, paired flop Js‑6h‑6d. This is an ideal spot to take the initiative — my range has more Jx and ace/face overcards than his, and the board has very few draws. I’ll make a small continuation bet (~1/3 pot) to generate fold equity against his missed Broadway and unpaired hands while keeping the pot manageable with only Ace‑high. If he folds I pick up the pot; if he calls I’ll reevaluate on the turn (blank turns I’ll often check back to control the pot, an Ace or Ten I can continue for value). If he raises, he’s likely to have a Jack or a set and I’ll concede since I have no pair and don’t want to bloat the pot deep. Bet size also gives him roughly 4:1 pot odds to call, so he must have decent equity to continue — exactly where I want to pressure him.

Heads-up on flop with top pair top kicker effectively (Jd9s, Js on board) on paired 66 board, a strong holding OOP vs Minni's wide button c-bet range. Her small 200 bet into 800 pot (25% pot) is standard light c-betting from her aggressive history, polarizes towards bluffs/missed draws (Ax, broadways, suited connectors like 78/57) or weak value (77-55, worse Js). Calling realizes equity against her wide range (~70% equity vs typical c-bet range), keeps pot controlled OOP with deep effective stacks (her 9.3k), induces turns bluffs from worse, and sets up raise/fold equity later if board worsens. Folding too tight; check-raising risks bloating pot vs better or folding out too much dead money/equity. Plan: Call small c-bet, re-eval turn (c/c/raise strong, fold weakness vs aggression).

Heads-up on turn with top pair (Jd pairing Js) and weak 9 kicker on a paired 66 board that hit the 5d turn, introducing diamond flush draws, open-enders (e.g., 47, 78), and gutshots while completing some straights. Pot 1000, OOP vs Minni's aggressive button range (wide: Ax, broadways, suited connectors, small pairs from history of light opens/cbets). Flop call was correct with ~65-70% equity vs her polarized c-bet range; turn worsens board but my hand retains showdown value vs bluffs/missed draws (KQ, AT, 78, diamonds without 6/J) and blocks some Jx. Her small flop c-bet (200 into 800) was likely light (~60% freq based on patterns); expect similar turn aggression but pot control key OOP with deep stacks (her 9.3k effective). Donking bloats pot OOP vs better Jx/sets/draws and denies her fold equity on bluffs; folding equity low with scary board. Checking induces bluffs/value from worse (AJ/KJ/JT, 77-44, floats), controls pot, realizes equity (~55-60% vs turn range), sets up check-raise vs strong bets or check-call weakness. Plan: Check, call small-medium bets, raise large/overbets if shows strength, fold to multi-street aggression without improvement. Long-term stack maximization favors controlled play.

Board: Js 6h 6d 5d. I have Ad Tc (Ace‑high). I c‑bet small on the flop and Elon called, and he now checks the turn — a blank for my hand. I have no pair and very little showdown value versus his calling range (likely Jx, 6x and some middling hands). Betting the turn would build a pot I’m usually behind in and will mostly get called by hands that already beat me; it also risks a check‑raise from sets. Elon is deep and tends to call wide, so my fold equity is thin. In position I prioritize pot control: check back to see the river for a real improvement (Ace or Ten) or a cleaner bluffing spot on safe runouts. If he leads out on the river I’ll fold most non‑thin value lines. Therefore I check and keep the pot manageable.

River Ks completes potential straights (e.g., T7 for 5-T straight? Wait, board 5-6-J-K with paired 6s: actually Q-T makes Broadway straight (T-J-Q-K-A no, board has no Q/T; 5-6-7-8 no 7/8; but K brings top pair possibilities and overcard). My Jd9s is top pair Js with weak 9 kicker (blocks JT/9x minimally), showdown value vs bluffs/missed draws (diamonds like AdXd without 6/J, Ax high, K high broadways like KQ/KT that checked turn fearing 6x/Jx, weaker floats). Paired board caps trips/boats somewhat (66 quads unlikely, full houses 665/565/K65 etc. <10% range). Minni's button range wide pre (suited connectors, Ax, broadways, pairs per history), c-bet flop small (light ~60-70% freq), called turn check (weakness? or trapping value like 66/J6/J5/set). Her turn check after my check indicates possible missed draw/weak value, less likely monster (would bet turn for value/protection). OOP pot control paramount with deep stacks (my 29k > her 9.3k), realizing equity (~55-60% vs polarized river range: bluffs/value thin). Betting polarizes, risks raise/fold equity loss vs better Jx (AJ/KJ/QJ)/Kx/set, bloats pot OOP vs calling station tendencies (her history calls stations but folds resistance). Checking induces bluffs/value bets from worse (Kx, Ad flush draws, Q high, 77-44 overpairs beaten by 6s/Js), allows cheap showdown vs air, sets up check-raise if overbets strength. Folding equity low if bet small; too thin for value. Long-term EV: check maximizes by balancing pot control, bluff induction, equity realization in heads-up deep vs aggressive opponent. Plan: Check, call small-medium value/thin bets (Jx/Kx), fold to large/overbet aggression indicating nuts.

I check. I have AdTc (only Ace‑high) on Js‑6h‑6d‑5d‑Ks. Elon called my small flop bet and then played very passively (check on turn and river), so his range contains a lot of hands that beat me (Jx, 6x, medium pocket pairs, occasional Kx) and also some marginal showdown hands. I have very little value to extract and my Ace only removes a few bluff combos, while Elon’s tendency to call down deep reduces my fold equity on a river bluff. Betting risks building a pot I’m usually behind in and opening myself to a check‑raise or thin calls from hands that beat me; pot control is the higher EV line. I’ll check to take the showdown and fold to meaningful aggression rather than bloating the pot with a weak bluff/value bet.

Heads-up on the button (SB) with Q9o, a solid hand in this spot. Standard to open-raise wide from this position to apply pressure and take initiative, especially deep-stacked. Minni in BB defends wide based on history, but we have post-flop position and fold equity. Raising to 3x BB (300 total) is optimal size for value/protection and pot control.

Heads-up in the big blind with K♦Q♣ facing a button open to 300. KQ offsuit is a strong, playable hand heads-up and has good equity vs a wide opening range. I want to take the initiative and apply pressure (fold equity + value) rather than just limping in out of position. I’ll 3-bet to 1,200 total — a moderate size that forces some weaker hands to fold, gets value from worse kings/queens/broadways, but doesn’t commit an excessive portion of my 9,210 stack. Plan if action continues: and Elon folds I scoop the 400 pot; if he calls I’ll play the flop cautiously out of position (c-bet favorable K/Q or drawy flops, check/reevaluate when an Ace or heavy action shows up); if he 4-bets large (commits a big fraction of stacks) I’ll fold KQ to avoid a marginal all-in situation.

Qd9h is a marginal but callable hand facing a BB 3-bet after my button open-raise. Minni's history shows aggressive defending and 3-betting wide from the blinds, so her range includes many Ax, broadway, suited hands, and bluffs. In position with deep effective stacks (her 8110), I get excellent implied odds and position postflop. Equity realization is strong heads-up OOP for her. Pot odds are favorable: calling 900 into 1500 (~25% equity needed, and we have ~35-45% vs typical 3-bet range). Folding is too tight; 4-betting is poor as it's not polarized well here without stronger blockers. Calling keeps pot manageable and exploits position.

I 3-bet preflop to 1,200 with K♦Q♣ and Elon called; pot is 2,400 on a Js 2s Th flop. I have two overcards and a backdoor Broadway (an A completes a broadway), but no spade/flush draw and no pair — this is a good spot to take the initiative as the preflop aggressor. A small continuation bet (~1/3 pot) pressures his wide calling range, folds out weak pairs and unpaired hands, and preserves stack size against a likely caller with Jx/Tx/overpairs. I’ll size to 800 to keep the pot manageable and retain fold equity; if he raises big I’ll respect that strength and fold, if he calls I’ll re-evaluate the turn (barrel A/K or check behind on heavy action).

Facing a continuation bet of 800 into 2400 from Minni's strong BB 3-bet range on a J-high flop with spade draw and straight possibilities. My Qd9h holds only a weak gutshot straight draw to the 8 (4 outs, ~8-10% equity) and no overcards that play well, no flush draw. Her range is polarized: strong made hands (JJ+, TT+, sets, two-pair) or strong draws (spades, open-enders like KJ/QJ/KQ). Pot odds require ~20% equity to call (800 into 4000), which I don't have sufficient vs her range (~15% max realized IP). Raising as a bluff is unexploitative here; Q9 blocks few value hands and doesn't make a strong semi-bluff. Folding preserves stack for better spots; history shows Minni cbets wide but her pre 3-bet narrows it enough to fold marginal gutshots IP deep.

Open-raise to seize initiative. Heads-up on the button KcTh is a strong opening hand — raising lets me isolate the BB, generate fold equity against a wide defending range, and play the postflop pot in position. A 3xBB size (300) is a standard, balanced raise: big enough to extract value and discourage limp/call junk, but small enough to keep the pot controllable with my stack. If called I will C-bet favorable K/T/A flops and use position to steer the hand; I will fold to very large 3-bets that commit a disproportionate part of my stack and continue selectively against smaller 3-bets.

Heads-up against Minni on the button who raises frequently from a wide range. My 5d6h is a speculative connected hand with straight potential. Pot odds are excellent: calling 200 more into a 400 pot gives about 3:1 odds (33% equity threshold), but with postflop playability, live cards, and deep stacks (my 29k vs her 10k), defending is +EV. Folding is too tight heads-up; raising is unnecessary with this hand as it plays well multiway but here OOP postflop. Prefer calling to realize equity and control pot size OOP.

Flop of Ad 9h Ts completely misses my 5d6h: no pair, no flush draw, only a weak gutshot straight draw to 78 for 56789. Button raiser Minni (10k stack) has a wide preflop range that connects well here with Ax, Tx, 9x, broadways, and occasional sets. My equity is low (~20-25% vs. her range), and being OOP with no strong draw or showdown value, betting as a bluff risks getting raised/folded out or called by better. Deep effective stacks (hers 10k into 600 pot), but check-fold is standard GTO-ish play to realize fold equity indirectly if she bets air. Defending wide OOP preflop was fine for playability, but this texture favors checking.

I hold KcTh — top pair (tens) with a strong king kicker on a  Ad‑9h‑Ts board. Heads‑up I opened preflop so I still have initiative; Villain’s BB calling range contains a lot of Ax, broadways and weaker pairs. His check can be either pot‑control with an Ace or weakness with misses/broadways. I want to extract value from Kx, weaker tens and other hands that will call, while keeping the pot manageable against actual Aces. A half‑pot C‑bet (300 into 600) accomplishes that: it gets value, denies equity to backdoor cards and leaves room to fold to a large check‑raise. Plan if raised: fold to a very large, commitment‑style check‑raise; call small/medium raises and re‑evaluate on the turn, continue on non‑Ace turns and be cautious if an Ace shows up.

Heads-up OOP on Ad9hTs flop after calling preflop with speculative 5d6h. Board hits Minni's wide button range hard (A9s+, ATs+, T9s, broadway, sets), while I have only a weak gutshot straight draw (to 78) with ~15-20% equity vs. her polarized betting range. Her 1/3 pot bet (300 into 900) has good fold equity and continues her aggression. Pot odds require ~25% equity to call (300/1200), but I lack that, plus reverse implied odds OOP if I hit (she likely has Ax/draw), and no flush draw or strong showdown value. Raising as a bluff is -EV given her stack (~10k) and range strength. Check-fold is standard; folding realizes 0 equity now to preserve stack for better spots in deep tournament.

Heads-up on the button/SB with KJo, a strong hand in this spot. Standard to raise wide from SB vs BB for value, initiative, and position post-flop. Minni defends frequently based on history. Raising to 3x BB (300 total) is standard sizing. Effective stacks allow for post-flop play.

Heads-up vs Elon's open from the SB, 4h7h is a clear defend in the big blind. Calling costs 200 into a 400 pot (after my call pot ~600) so I need ~33% equity — 4♥7♥ has good playability and equity versus a wide SB opening range, plus deep stacks give strong implied odds. I don't want to 3-bet OOP with a marginal suited connector against an aggro, deep-stacked opener; calling preserves fold equity and lets me realize equity postflop. Plan: call to see a flop, proceed aggressively only if I hit (pairs/strong draws) and fold to heavy, persistent pressure on dry boards.

I called preflop with 4♥7♥ to see a flop and realized I’ve completely missed this one. On 9♦‑3♦‑J♥ I have no pair, only a remote backdoor heart flush (must hit runner‑runner — ~4% by river) and about ~24% to hit at least a pair by river. The flop heavily favors Elon's opening range (many Jx, diamond draws, overcards) and he’s demonstrated aggressive continuation betting. Betting out OOP here would be a pure bluff with low fold equity and a high likelihood of being raised or called by better hands. I’ll check to control pot size, realize equity cheaply, and be prepared to fold to meaningful pressure — only continue if I improve or can see a very cheap turn.

Top pair top kicker (Jacks with King kicker) on a flop with a diamond flush draw and low cards. This is a strong value hand heads-up with position. Minni checked, indicating weakness or trapping, but history shows she calls light but folds to aggression often. Standard continuation bet for value, to protect against draws (diamond flush draw, gutshots), and maintain initiative. Sizing at about 2/3 pot (400 into 600) balances value and fold equity while keeping pot manageable given her shorter stack (~10k). Avoid checking as it gives free cards to draws or allows bluffs.

Heads‑up I called preflop with 4♥7♥ and missed this 9♦‑3♦‑J♥ flop — I have only a runner‑runner heart flush (~4% to hit) and about ~24% to make at least a pair by the river. The pot is 1000 and Elon’s c‑bet is 400; calling 400 would make the pot 1800, so I need roughly 400/1800 ≈ 22.2% equity to break even. Given my improvement equity is around that threshold and Elon opens/continuation‑bets wide heads‑up, a flat call to realize equity is the best, least risky line. Raising OOP with this junk is a bluff that can get punished; folding is too tight with good pot odds and deep stacks. I'll call, see the turn, and fold to significant turn aggression unless I improve (pair/real draw), otherwise proceed only with cheap cards or a genuine improvement.

I’ve completely missed the turn with 4♥7♥. The 6♣ doesn’t help: a flush is impossible now (only one card to come) and I have only 6 outs (three 4s + three 7s) => 6/46 ≈ 13% to pair on the river. Pot is 1,400 and my stack is deep enough to avoid spewing chips OOP. Betting here would be a pure bluff with limited fold equity vs an aggressive opponent — for example a 700 turn bluff requires ≈33% fold frequency to break even (a 400 bet still needs ~22%), and Elon has shown willingness to call light. Best line is to control the pot and realize equity cheaply: check and evaluate on the river. If Elon bets a meaningful size I should fold (my equity is well below the needed threshold); if he checks back I get a free shot at a 4 or 7 on the river and can proceed accordingly.

TPTK on a draw-heavy board (diamond flush draw on flop, turn 6c bricks most draws but adds straight possibilities like 45/57 low). Minni checked flop and called my c-bet, now checks turn again, showing continued weakness. Her history: calls light pre/flop but folds to turn aggression frequently (e.g., hands 84,86,88,90). With position, deep stack advantage (28k vs her 10k), and pot 1400, a value bet extracts from her Ax, flush draws, lower pairs, or floats. Sizing ~2/3 pot (900) balances value vs worse made hands/draws, builds pot without overcommitting, and offers fold equity if she has nothing. Checking risks giving free river cards to draws or bluffs; betting maintains initiative and protects hand.

Fold. I hold 4♥7♥ on 9♦‑3♦‑J♥‑6♣ and have no made hand and no realistic draw: the backdoor heart draw is dead after the 6♣, leaving only six outs (three 4s + three 7s) ≈13% to pair on the river. The pot is 2300 and Elon bet 900, so a call requires ~28.1% equity (900 / (2300+900)), well above my ~13% chance — mathematically a -EV call. Elon’s line (open preflop, flop c‑bet, large turn barrel) is consistent with lots of value and semi‑bluffs that still beat my thin outs; raising would be a pure bluff with poor blockers and large reverse implied risk given his deeper stack. Preserve chips, wait for a better spot with real equity or position. Action: fold.

Heads-up on the button with 8♣8♦ (100bb effective) is a clear open-raise spot. Pocket eights are a strong, standalone hand heads-up and play very well in position. I want to build the pot, deny free cards to a wide BB range, and isolate Elon, so I’ll open to a standard 3x BB size rather than just completing. Raising to 300 total (commitment includes my SB) achieves that while keeping postflop playability. Plan: if Elon 3-bets a reasonable size I’ll continue in position; if he 4-bets shove or 3-bets to an extremely large size I’ll re-evaluate (likely fold vs an obvious nutted range).

Facing a button raise to 300 from Minni, who has been aggressive throughout the tournament. My hand is T5o (5c Th), a weak offsuit non-connector with poor playability postflop. In the big blind, while positionally challenged heads-up, calling 200 into a 400 pot offers insufficient equity and implied odds against her wide button range. Folding preserves my deep stack (29k) for better spots, avoiding speculative defense of marginal hands.

Heads-up preflop from SB/Button with 2s5d offsuit, one of the worst possible hands. No straight potential, no flush draw possible preflop, dominated by any playable hand. Pot odds to call 50 into 150 are decent (3:1), but equity against BB defending range is too low (~30% or less). Raising is bluff-only with no blockers. Standard fold to preserve stack and wait for better spots. Deep stacked, no need to speculate.

Heads-up on the button (small blind). K♣5♠ is a reasonable open in heads-up — it's often ahead of the BB's defending range and plays well in position. The pot is 150 and I only need to put 50 more to call, but I want initiative and fold equity, so I will raise to 300 (≈3x BB). Size is big enough to fold many marginal BB hands but small relative to stacks, so it preserves maneuverability. Plan: if called, take the lead and continuation-bet dry boards; if Elon 3-bets large, fold K5o (we're dominated), only consider calling very small 3-bets for pot odds.

Heads-up situation with deep stacks (297bb for me, 99bb for Minni). Qd6s is offsuit queen-high, but in BB vs BTN/SB open to 3bb, the range is very wide (~60%+ of hands). Equity ~42% vs such a range, and pot odds are excellent: 200 to call into 400 pot (~33% needed). Postflop I have position as BB acts last. Prefer calling over folding (too tight) or 3-betting (Q6o is a mediocre bluff candidate without strong blockers or playability, better to realize equity). Historical play shows Minni aggressive but calls stations sometimes; see flop and play postflop.

Flop board is 2s7s8h, highly coordinated with spade flush draw, open-enders (5-9, 6-9, 9-T, etc.), and pairs. My Qd6s is queen-high with a weak gutshot straight draw (to the 5 for 5-6-7-8) and backdoor spade (6s blocks some), but no strong equity (~30-40% vs Minni's button opening range which includes many draws, pairs, broadways). Out of position as BB, pot 600, acting first. Betting risks raising into better or getting check-raised; check-raise bluff possible but Q6o lacks strong blockers/playability, better spots exist. Minni aggressive preflop from button, likely continuation bets wide (~60-70%). Plan: check-fold to most bets, controlling pot and realizing fold equity pre was correct but postflop no showdown value or implied odds justify calling. Folding to check maximizes EV long-term.

I was the preflop raiser (300) and have position — standard heads-up strategy is to continue taking the initiative. Flop 2♠-7♠-8♥ is somewhat connected and has two spades, but a large portion of Elon's calling range (overcards, weak broadways, many offsuit hands and small pairs) misses. My K♣5♠ is only K-high but as the aggressor I have fold equity and want to deny equity to spade and straight draw combos. A bet of about 2/3 pot (400 into 600) applies pressure to his marginal hands, keeps the pot manageable given deep stacks, and preserves easy folding to a large raise. Plan: fold to a big raise, call a reasonable single raise only with pot-odds justification, and re-evaluate on the turn if called.

Heads-up on flop 2s7s8h, highly coordinated board with flush draws and open-enders. Qd6s has queen-high, weak gutshot to the 5 (4 outs), and backdoor spade draw. OOP after checking, facing 400 bet into 1000 pot (~28% pot bet). While pot odds require ~22% equity, my equity vs Minni's wide button c-bet range (draws, pairs, bluffs, Ax suited) is marginal (~25-35%), but playability is poor OOP with no strong draw or showdown value. Calling invites difficult turn decisions, especially deep-stacked (Minni 95bb). History shows Minni aggressive postflop but capable of value betting; check-fold realizes EV by avoiding tough spots, consistent with preflop plan to control pot and fold to aggression without improvement. Raising is a bluff too thin without better blockers.

T5o is a trash hand: offsuit, gapped, poor post-flop playability. In heads-up from SB/Button vs BB, opening ranges are wide (suited broadways, connectors, Ax), but T5o equity vs calling range is low (~35-40% vs tight defense, worse if wide). Minni has shown tendency to defend/raise, but folding weak hands maximizes EV long-term in tournament. Pot odds poor for limp (adds dead money but weak OOP post-flop). Preserve 29k stack vs her 10k for premium spots. Fold.

Heads-up on the button (I posted the SB 50) with 7♥8♣ offsuit. Against a single opponent and deep stacks (~105 BB effective) this is a standard open-raise spot: the hand has good playability and straight potential and I want to take the initiative and use position postflop. I will raise to 3x the big blind (300 total) to gain fold equity and build a pot when I hit, while keeping the option to continue aggressively in position. Plan: fold to a large, committed 3-bet; consider calling a smaller 3-bet to play postflop in position; if Elon simply calls, use c-bets and positional advantage to pressure his range.

5s6c is offsuit low connectors, a marginal speculative hand at best. Facing a button raise from Minni, who is aggressive but in position. Out of position post-flop with no initiative, poor playability, and low equity against her wide range. Pot odds are 2:1 but implied odds insufficient OOP with this hand. Standard fold to preserve stack.

Heads-up pre-flop from SB with A9h suited, a strong hand with good playability, high card strength, and suited potential. Pot is 150 (SB 50 + BB 100), to call 50 more to complete. Raising is standard to take initiative, deny equity to worse hands, and build the pot when ahead in equity. Minni's stack is 10k, mine 29k, deep enough for post-flop play. Raising to 2.5x BB (250 total) is balanced size, min raise is 200, but slightly larger pressures her wide calling range while keeping pot manageable.

Heads-up in the big blind vs a button open to 250. I hold A♥4♠ — a hand worth defending widely heads-up. Pot is 350 and calling 150 more (total 250) gives me ~30% break-even equity against a likely wide opening range from Elon. My stack is deep, so calling is cheap and keeps his bluffs and worse aces in the pot; a min-raise to 400 would bloat the pot out of position with a marginal hand and isn’t necessary. Plan: call to see the flop — proceed aggressively if I pair my Ace or pick up strong equity, otherwise proceed cautiously OOP.

Check. Heads-up on K♥ J♦ 2♣ with A♥4♠ — I have only Ace-high but decent showdown value and an Ace blocker vs Elon's wide button opening. Out of position I don't want to inflate the pot with a marginal hand; checking keeps pot control and lets Elon c-bet bluffs and thinner value hands. Pot is 500 and stacks are deep (I ~10.5k, he ~29k), so plan is: check and call small-to-medium continuation bets (~≤ half pot) to realize equity and re-evaluate on the turn; fold to very large overbets that price me out or represent clear two-pair+/nut hands. If he checks back I can take a free card or apply targeted turn aggression; if I pair my Ace or pick up substantial equity I'll switch to a more aggressive line (lead or raise for value).

Preflop as BTN/SB raiser with Ad9h, a strong ace with backdoor potential, against BB caller's wide range. Flop 2c Kh Jd is somewhat coordinated but rainbow, favoring my range heavily as preflop raiser (AK/AQ/AJ/KQ/KT/JT etc.). Minni checks, giving me free initiative. A9 high has good blockers to top pairs, decent equity vs her calling range (~40-50% vs random broadway/pairs), and position. C-bet 60% pot (300 into 500) for thin value vs Ax/Kx worse, protection vs draws (KQ/QT), and fold equity vs air. Her stack 10k allows multi-street play if called. Checking risks giving free card to overcards/draws, suboptimal given range advantage. History shows Minni calls light but folds to aggression often postflop.

Heads‑up OOP with A♥4♠ on K♥ J♦ 2♣. Preflop I defended and he continuation‑bet 300 into a 500 pot (pot now 800); calling 300 commits a small amount to try and realize equity (300/(800+300) ≈ 27%). My A♥ has good showdown value and blocks a number of strong A‑combos, and versus a wide BTN opening/c‑bet range I expect to have roughly break‑even or better equity. Raising out of position with only Ace‑high is thin — it bloats the pot with marginal equity and invites difficult decisions on later streets — and folding would be too nitty given the price and blocker value. Plan: call 300 to keep weaker Ax and bluffs in his range, then re‑evaluate on the turn (bet/raise if I pair the Ace or face weak action; fold to very large turn bets that clearly represent two‑pair+ or strong value).

Elon checked the turn on 2♣ K♥ J♦ J♥ after c‑betting the flop; I hold A♥4♠. I called the flop to keep his weaker Ax and bluffs in and the turn check looks like pot‑control or weakness. With the Ace blocker and reasonable showdown value, a small‑to‑medium bluff here can often take the pot now. The pot is 1,100; betting 600 (~55% pot) requires ~35% fold equity to be profitable, which I expect against his checking range. If he calls I still have some showdown value and will re‑evaluate the river; if he check‑raises large (representing two‑pair/trips) I’ll fold — I won’t bloat the pot with only Ace‑high. Betting 600 applies pressure while keeping the decision manageable on later streets.

Facing a turn lead of 600 into 1700 after Minni called my flop c-bet on a K-J-2 rainbow board. My Ad9h is ace-high with no pair, no strong draw (minor backdoor heart flush potential but irrelevant). The turn Jh pairs the Jd, completing straights (QT) and adding flush draws (hearts now on board with Kh Jh). As preflop raiser and flop bettor, my range includes AK/AQ/AJ/KQ/KJ etc., but Minni's call on flop and now turn lead suggests strength: likely Kx (top pair), Jx for trips/two pair, QT straight, or sets. Ace-high has ~20-30% equity vs her polarized leading range, poor implied odds given her 9.5k stack vs my 28k (but pot odds 600 to 2300 is ~26%, not enough). History shows Minni calls light but turn leads often indicate value; folding weak ace-high is standard to avoid spew, preserving stack for better spots in heads-up play with position.

Heads-up on the button (SB) with K3o. In heads-up you should open very wide from the button — Kx is routinely a raising hand. Limping (calling the extra 50) is too passive: it gives the BB a cheap flop and surrenders initiative. I’ll open to the min-raise (200 total) to take the initiative, leverage positional advantage postflop, and apply pressure to Elon’s wider defending range. If he 3-bets big I will fold this marginal hand; versus a small 3-bet I can consider calling to use position, but I won’t get sticky into large pots with a weak kicker. Primary goal: steal the blind and play postflop in position on favorable terms.

Pocket Tens is a premium hand heads-up against a button raise. Minni's range from the button is very wide given the history of frequent raising from late position. Her raise to 200 is small, making this an ideal spot to 3-bet for value, isolate, and take control of the pot. Effective stacks are 11k, so a standard 3-bet size of 700 (about 3.5x) puts pressure while building the pot without overcommitting. Calling is fine pot-odds wise but allows her to realize equity with worse hands post-flop.

I opened wide to 200 on the button with K♠3♦ to take initiative in heads-up play. Elon’s 3-bet to 700 is a sizable reraise — I must call 500 more to win a 1,400 pot (call 500 into current 900), so break-even equity is ~35.7%. K3o is frequently dominated and has poor equity against a normal 3‑bet range; a 4‑bet is unjustified and calling will put me in awkward postflop spots with a weak kicker. I’ll fold, preserve my 11,060 stack, and wait for a better spot to exploit position and apply aggression.

Heads-up on the button/SB with 4d5d, suited connectors with great playability, straight and flush potential. Deep stacks relative to blinds. Minni in BB likely to defend wide but folding too often to aggression based on history. Standard to raise for initiative, steal blinds or build pot when called. Raising to 2.5x BB (250 total) is optimal size: not too small to get called by junk, not too large OOP. Folding trash, limping weak; raise exploits position.

Heads-up vs the button (Elon) who opened to 250 from the SB. I have K♥7♠ in the big blind with deep stacks (≈110/287 BB). This is a hand I want to see a flop with rather than 3-bet out of position — Elon’s open is wide so Kx performs well heads-up and the call is cheap: I need to put 150 more into a 350 pot (pot ~500 after my call), roughly 3.3:1 pot odds (~23% equity). A min 3-bet to 400 would bloat the pot OOP with limited fold equity, so I’ll call, see the flop, and decide postflop (barrell for value if I hit top pair or fold/defend carefully on highly coordinated boards).

I’m OOP with K♥7♠ on a dry Q♠-6♣-4♥ board. Elon opened wide heads‑up preflop so his range will c‑bet a lot, but my hand has only one overcard and modest showdown value — not worth bloating the pot out of position with a probe. I’ll check to control the pot, induce bluffs/c-bets, and realize equity. Plan: call small-to-medium continuation bets (roughly up to ~1/2 pot ≈ 250) and fold to large overbets/raises; if he checks back we proceed cheaply to the turn and can value-bet a King or fold to heavy action later. Check keeps my options and avoids committing too many chips with a marginal hand OOP.

Flopped bottom pair (4s) with a gutshot straight draw (to 3 or 7) and backdoor diamond flush draw, giving strong playability and equity heads-up (~35-45% vs typical calling range). Board has some straight draw potential but no completed strong draws likely yet. Minni checked OOP after calling preflop raise, representing a wide range including Qx, 6x, Ax, suited connectors, draws. As aggressor in position (acting first postflop but with initiative), standard c-bet to protect pair, deny equity to floats/draws/OESD, and extract value from worse made hands or draws. Bet ~70% pot (350) balances value/protection/semi-bluff potential; fold equity high if she misses. Check would be too passive, risking free cards to her draws or letting her rep strength.

I checked the dry Q♠-6♣-4♥ flop to control the pot with K♥7♠. Elon now fires 350 into the ~500 pot (pot = 850). Calling 350 to win 1,200 requires ≈29.2% equity. My hand’s realistic ways to improve are pairing the K or the 7 (3 outs each, combined ≈24% to hit by the river — runner-runner straights are negligible). There are no meaningful flush draws here with my single suited card and the current board, so I don’t have the equity to justify a call. Raising OOP with such a marginal hand also has poor risk/return. Given the large sizing, OOP position, and my preflop plan to only call smaller c-bets, this is a fold to preserve chips for better spots.

Heads-up on the button with 3♠2♠ — this is a very playable hand in position: suited, connected, lots of postflop playability and blocker value. The pot is 150 and I only have to call 50, but I prefer to apply pressure and build the pot with position rather than limp. I’ll open-raise to 3x the big blind (300 total) to generate fold equity and define Elon’s range. If called I plan to use position to c-bet on favorable flops (and realize equity on draws); if Elon 3-bets large (well above ~3x my open) I’ll concede and fold this marginal hand, but I may call a smaller 3-bet to see a flop given deep stacks and implied odds. Total committed with this action: 300 chips.

2d Th is a very weak offsuit hand with poor playability, low equity against a button raise range. Minni raises frequently from late position as shown in history (e.g., Hands 51,53,54,60, etc.), likely stealing blinds. I'm out of position postflop, pot odds are marginal (3:1 for 200 into 600), but implied odds are poor with this hand. Folding preserves my deep stack (29k) for better spots against her shorter stack (10k). No reason to defend trash here.

Heads-up on the button/SB with a massive stack advantage (29k vs 10k). 3h4h is a premium speculative hand heads-up: suited, connected, great playability for straights/flushes, equity realization in position post-flop. Standard to raise for initiative, fold equity (Minni folds frequently to preflop raises in history), or build pot OOP cheaply if called. Raising 3x BB (300 total) is balanced aggression, denies her equity, and maximizes EV long-term in tournament.

Fold. Facing a button open to 300, my hand 9♣2♠ is among the worst holdings — very low equity and dominated often. Pot is 400 and calling 200 gives only 2:1 pot odds (~33% equity needed), which 9-2 offsuit doesn't have vs a typical button opening range, and I'd be out of position postflop. No value in a 3-bet bluff here given stack dynamics; preserve chips for better spots.

Heads-up on the button with 2♠4♣ — a very weak hand, but the price to see the flop is tiny. I’ve already posted the 50 SB, so calling the additional 50 (total contribution 100) gives me position and good pot odds (50 to win a 200 pot → 25%). I’ll call and keep the pot small; I won’t open-raise with 2-4o since facing a 3-bet from Elon's deep, often-aggressive range would be costly. Postflop plan: play straightforwardly — fold to strong aggression if I miss, continue only if I pair/improve or pick up clear equity.

Heads-up pre-flop in BB vs SB limp. Pot is 200, I've posted the BB. Ac5o is a strong playable hand heads-up: ace-high blocker, live ace, straight potential with 2-3-4-6 or wheel possibilities. SB limp range is wide, so raising takes down the pot often or builds it when called, gaining initiative post-flop. Min raise is to 200, but standard to raise to ~3x BB or pot-ish. Raising to 300 total (adding 200) applies pressure on her shorter stack (10k vs my 29k). Folding or checking too weak; this spot is profitable long-term.

I’m on the button with 2♠4♣. I already posted the 50 SB and called the extra 50 earlier (I’ve contributed 100); Elon raised to 300 so I’m facing 200 to call. Stacks are deep (I have 10,710, he has 28,890) and the pot is 400; calling 200 makes the pot 600 so break-even equity is ~33%. 2‑4o is a very weak hand, but heads‑up my positional advantage and Elon’s frequent wide opening range make a call reasonable — the absolute cost is small and I can fold to strong postflop aggression. I will not 3‑bet with this holding (too weak) and folding surrenders the button unnecessarily. Postflop plan: play straightforward in position — check/control on dry high cards and fold to heavy pressure if I miss, call small continuation bets when I have backdoor equity or a marginal pair, and only apply pressure if I improve or see clear weakness. Call 200 to see the flop and keep the pot manageable.

Heads-up pot on a draw-heavy flop (spade flush draw, possible straight draws like 56, 89, JQ gutshots). I have Ac5h: pure ace-high, no pair, no flush draw, weak gutshot to 6 for 45-6-7-10 (not clean), and backdoor heart draw at best. OOP in BB vs Button who limped SB and called my raise (wide range: suited connectors, Ax suited/weak, pocket pairs, broadways). Betting thin ace-high risks folding out better or getting called/raised by draws/hits. Pot control is key; check to induce bluffs from her wide range or take free turn card. If she checks back, great; if bets, ace-blocker allows hero call in position or fold most. Checking retains fold equity post-flop and avoids bloating pot OOP with marginal hand. Long-term EV+ as villain shows passive tendencies in history (folds to c-bets often but chases draws).

I hold 2♠4♣ on 7♠–4♠–10♦ and have a single pair (pair of 4s). This is a marginal, vulnerable hand — there are many turn/river cards that beat me (10s, 7s, any overpair or pocket pair) and the two spades on board make spade combos dangerous. Elon checked after his preflop raise, so I prefer pot control and using position to see a free turn rather than building a pot with a thin value bet that can be raised off me. Plan: check to keep the pot manageable, call small turn bets to realize equity, and fold to heavy aggression unless I improve to two pair/trips or pick up a clearer line to extract value.

Heads-up on turn with 28k effective (villain 10k). Pot 600. Board 7s4sTd9d: draw-heavy (spade flush draw, diamond flush draw now, gutshots like J8, 65). Ac5h is ace-high with backdoor heart draw, blocks strong Ax hands. Preflop I raised her limp-call (wide range: suited connectors, Ax, pairs 22-99, broadways). Flop check-check suggests she capped her range somewhat (missed draws/pairs avoiding bet OOP into c-bet spot). 9d turn is blank for me, completes some diamond draws but doesn't hit many strong hands directly; increases volatility. OOP with marginal ace-high, betting thin value risks: worse hands fold (bluffs/draws check behind), better call/raise (sets, two-pair+). Pot control key: check to induce bluffs from her missed draws/weak pairs or get free river. If she bets, ace-blocker good for calling stationary vs her history (passive check-backs, folds to aggression often but chases sometimes). EV+ long-term to check, avoid bloating pot OOP where I'm vulnerable. No strong reason to bet given dynamics and villain tendencies from history.

Check. I have a marginal one-pair (pair of 4s) with a weak kicker and a single spade. Elon opened wide preflop but has been passive (checked flop and turn), so his range is wide and may include many overcards or medium pairs — many hands still beat me (tens, sevens, higher pairs). With the pot only 600 and deep effective stacks, pot control is the right play: betting thin here risks getting raised off my vulnerable hand or bloating the pot facing a better holding. I’ll check to see the river for free. Plan for the river: if I improve to two-pair/trips I’ll bet for value; if a safe blank comes and Elon checks I’ll consider a small value bet or check through to showdown; if the river brings a dangerous diamond or big card and Elon leads strongly I will fold to large aggression.

River OOP heads-up with Ac5h (ace-high blocker) on a 7s4sTd9dJc board. Pot 600, villain 10.5k effective. Preflop: raised her SB limp-call (wide range: suited gappers/connectors like 56s-89s spades/diamonds, weak Ax, small pairs 22-66/88/99, broadways suited). Flop/turn check-check indicates she missed primary draws (spade flush draw, diamond flush draw added turn, OESD/GSD like J8/JQ/56/65/85) or has capped weak value (underpairs like 55/66, stubborn Ax). River Jc is a blank for most missed draws but completes nut straight 78 (78s/o best fits her range: connected/spade-capable). However, 78 too narrow for her limped-called-then-checked range; more likely weak showdown hands or missed draws that can bluff. Betting ace-high thin value poor: worse hands (missed draws/bluffs) fold, better (78, sets, two-pair like T9/JT/97) snap-call/raise; no fold equity vs passive villain history (checks behind often, folds to bets but rarely bluffs rivers). Pot control maximized equity: check induces bluffs from her overcards/draws or cheap showdown vs worse ace-high/underpairs. Ace blocker defends well if she bets (call stations like her call wide per history: e.g., Hand 81 called river with weak). Long-term EV+ check > bet/raise given dynamics, villain passivity (folds aggression but chases value), board texture favoring showdown.

Check (amount 0). I only have a tiny one-pair (4s) with a terrible kicker (2). Villain opened wide preflop but has been passive (checked flop and turn), so his range contains many hands that beat me (Jx, Tx, 9x, higher pairs and possible 8x straights). A river bet is unlikely to get called by worse hands enough to be +EV, and it risks being raised off my marginal holding. Pot control and taking the free showdown is the correct play here — preserve stack and see his hand. If he had led the river I would seriously consider folding to large aggression; since he checked I’ll check through.

Heads-up on the button (SB) with deep stacks and position advantage. Even with a weak offsuit hand like 2s5c, the standard strategy is to raise a wide range (70%+) from the button in heads-up play to steal the BB and dead money or play post-flop in position. 25o has some backdoor straight potential and fold equity against Minni's wide defending range observed in prior hands. Pot odds for a limp are decent but limping invites multiway trouble; raising applies pressure. Raising to 3x (300 total) is standard sizing. Minni's shorter stack (110BB) doesn't change this dynamic significantly.

Call. In heads-up play the opener's range is very wide and calling 200 more (300 total committed including my BB) gives me ~33% pot odds to see a flop. 3♣2♦ is a weak hand, so I don't want to 3‑bet out of position as a big bluff or fold away a lot of dead money; calling keeps the pot small and lets me realize equity. Post-flop plan: proceed cautiously — fold to heavy, well-sized aggression on dry boards if I miss; continue or raise if I pair, pick up a straight/flush draw, or get favorable action.

I hold 3♣2♦ on a 7♠ 9♥ T♥ flop — no pair, no meaningful draw. I acted cautiously preflop and this is a dry, unfavorable board for my hand. Betting out OOP here would be a pure bluff with limited fold equity versus an opener who raised preflop and will often call small probes; with deep stacks that’s unnecessarily risky. Best line is to control the pot and give up if Elon fires. I’ll check and fold to reasonable continuation bets; if he checks back I can see a free turn and re-evaluate.

Heads-up on the flop with position after my pre-flop raise. Board is 7s9hTh, very draw-heavy with straight and flush draw potential, favoring my perceived range as the pre-flop raiser. My 2s5c is pure air with no pair, draw, or showdown value, but in position vs a checking BB defender, a continuation bet exploits fold equity against Minni's wide calling range (which often includes marginal hands without top pair or better). Minni's check suggests weakness or a weak draw, and betting denies equity while building the pot when I hit later. Sizing to 2/3 pot (400 into 600) balances value/protection while keeping fold equity high given her ~100BB stack. Checking back is too passive, inviting free cards to her draws. This wide c-betting strategy maximizes EV over many hands.

Fold. I flat-called preflop to see a flop, but 3♣2♦ completely misses the 7♠ 9♥ T♥ board — no pair, no backdoor flush and only 4 immediate outs (two 3s, two 2s). Elon’s c-bet is 400 into a 1000 pot; calling 400 to win 1400 requires ~28.6% equity, while my chance to improve by the river is only ~16.5%. Out of position with a rag hand against a wide but still often-cbetting BTN and deep stacks, calling invites further pressure and a bluff-raise is unlikely to get sufficient fold equity. Best play is to fold and wait for a better spot.

Heads-up with K♦Q♥ on the button (I’ve already posted the SB) is a strong open-raise spot. I have positional advantage postflop and deep stacks (~107 BB), so I want to seize initiative, build the pot for value, and deny Elon cheap equity. A standard 3xBB open to 300 fits the table’s sizing norms here and gives good fold equity while leaving room to maneuver if Elon 3-bets. I’ll raise to 300 total (i.e. commit 250 more on top of my 50 SB); if he 3-bets I’ll re-evaluate based on sizing and range, but vs a call I’ll play postflop in position for value.

Heads-up preflop in BB facing a button/SB raise to 300 (call 200 into 400 pot). Qs8d is offsuit queen-high with mediocre playability OOP. Against a wide button range, it has some equity (~35-40%) but is often dominated by better queens or stronger hands. Postflop OOP with poor implied odds on paired boards or uncoordinated flops, calling risks tough spots. 3-betting as a bluff with blockers but no strong history or fold equity guarantee given opponent's stack (10k). My deep stack (29k) allows waiting for premium hands or better defense spots. Folding preserves stack and avoids marginal situations.

AQo is a premium hand heads-up, especially on the button/SB with position postflop. Minni has been aggressive but folds frequently to raises preflop. Raising standard size to 3x the BB (300 total) to take initiative, build the pot, and apply pressure. Effective stacks are deep but her shorter stack (10k) vs my 29k favors playing strong hands aggressively. Folding or limping is suboptimal.

Heads-up vs Elon (button/SB) who opened to 300. I’m in the big blind with K♠J♦ — a strong heads-up holding that fares well against a wide SB open. Stacks are deep (I ~108 BB, he ~288 BB), so I can apply pressure without committing too much. I want to both get value from worse broadways/pairs and earn fold equity against his wider opening range, so I’ll 3-bet rather than just call out of position. I’ll raise to 900 (≈3x his open): big enough to fold out weaker holdings and define his range, but small enough not to bloat the pot unnecessarily. Plan: fold to a large, polarized 4-bet; if he calls I’ll c-bet favorable flops and control pot size on coordinated/very bad boards. Calling (300) keeps me OOP with initiative to him postflop, but 3-betting here maximizes expected value against a wide opener.

AQo is a premium hand heads-up with position. Minni's 3-bet sizing is standard but her history shows she often folds to further aggression (e.g., Hand 90, 91). Folding is too tight; flat-calling risks playing OOP postflop without initiative and allows her bluffs/draws to realize equity cheaply. 4-betting isolates her shorter stack (10k effective), builds a big pot with equity advantage (dominates many 3-bet ranges like Axs, KQs, lower pairs), and has fold equity given her tendencies. Sizing to ~2.7x her raise (2400 total) pressures without overcommitting; if she shoves, we have ~35-40% equity vs her range and can call profitably with dead money. Maximizes EV long-term.

I 3-bet to 900 with K♠J♦ to apply pressure and isolate Elon’s wide SB opening. He responds with a sizable 4-bet to 2400 — a polarized sizing that, given his earlier aggression, is weighted toward premium value (AA/KK/QQ/AK) with only occasional bluffs. KJ offsuit is a decent heads-up hand but is often dominated by that value-heavy 4-bet range and plays poorly out of position postflop. Calling 1500 into a 3300 pot requires roughly 31% equity (1500/(3300+1500) ≈ 31.25%); versus a tight, value-polarized 4-bet range I won’t have that. 5-betting or inflating the pot to 3900+ with KJ would commit a large chunk of my stack with a marginal hand and is exploitable by his value holdings. Preserve stack and pick better postflop spots — fold here.

Heads-up on the button with Td3d (suited but very weak) and ~100bb effective. Pot is 150 and it only costs 50 to see a flop. I prefer to call here: keep the pot small, use my positional advantage postflop, and avoid building a bigger pot by open‑raising into an opponent who defends and 3‑bets often. Plan: see the flop cheaply, continue only with a pair, strong draw, or favorable runout; otherwise fold to heavy action. A raise to 200 is an option as a steal but is too risky with this hand and stack depth.

Heads-up in the big blind facing a small blind limp from Minni. My hand is 4d5d, suited connectors with good playability. Pot is 200 with no additional investment needed to see a flop. Raising preflop risks playing out of position post-flop with a speculative hand that benefits from multi-way pots or set-mining dynamics, but here it's cheap to realize equity. Minni's limp suggests a wide range, and checking keeps the pot small while maintaining position post-flop (acting last as BB). No fold equity needed; proceed to flop.

Heads-up on the flop with 4d5d facing a limper's range from SB. The board 8c-6h-7c gives me an open-ended straight draw (4-5 for 3 or 9 to make the nut straight), plus backdoor diamond flush draw, for massive equity (~35-45% against typical calling ranges). OOP as BB but initiative from checking pre. Minni's limp range is wide (pairs, suited connectors, Ax suited, etc.), many of which hit this board medium strength (77,66,87s,76s, flush draws). Betting semi-bluffs for value from weaker draws/pairs, fold equity vs misses, protection vs her draws/improvers, and pot-building since I have the best draw. Check risks giving free card to her equity or check-raise spot, but betting takes control. Size ~3/4 pot (150 into 200) balances value/fold equity without overbetting deep.

Call. I'm on the button with Td3d on 8c-6h-7c and Elon led a small 150 into the pot (pot shown 350). I have an overcard (T), a 4‑out straight to the 9 and backdoor diamonds — plus positional advantage. Calling 150 to potentially win ~500 gives me ~30% pot‑odds; against a wide heads‑up c‑betting range my hand has roughly that equity and good implied odds to realize it. Raising here would be a thin, high‑variance bluff into a deeper-stacked, aggressive opponent; I prefer to keep the pot controllable, see the turn, and fold to heavy turn aggression unless I pair or complete my draw (if I hit a 9 or T I will apply pressure).

Turn board Js is a blank for my hand, keeping my open-ended straight draw live with 8 outs (~17% equity). It may complete some straight hands like 45 (nuts) or improve Jx pairs/draws in Minni's limp-call range (pairs, weak draws, medium strength hands from flop callers). OOP after flop c-bet/call, checking realizes equity cheaply deep-stacked (her 97bb, me 297bb), induces bluffs from her missed draws/weak pairs, allows check-raise semi-bluff if she bets, and avoids bloating pot OOP with a draw when she can raise or float. Betting risks facing raise without fold equity vs strong hands or getting check-raised; pot control preferred over thin value semi-bluff here. Consistent with speculative hand dynamics.

Elon checked the turn after a small flop stab — I missed with Td3d and only have 7 clean outs to improve by the river (3 tens + 4 nines) plus remote backdoor diamonds. Pot is ~500 with ~100bb effective; this is a marginal spot and my prior plan was explicitly to keep the pot small unless I hit. Betting here would be a thin bluff that can be called or raised by many hands (pairs, Js, 8s) and needlessly bloats the pot. I’ll check to realize my equity for free and only apply pressure if the river pairs my Ten or completes the 9 (then I can bet/raise for value); if the river misses and Elon bets, I’ll fold to meaningful aggression.

River 9h completes my straight with 4d5d for 4-5-6-7-8, a very strong hand (nuts unless opponent has exactly a 5x for the superior 5-6-7-8-9 straight, which is unlikely from her SB limp-call flop range). Board brings possible club flushes (live on flop) or unlikely heart flushes, but my hand is ahead of pairs (88/77/66 top/middle/set), missed draws, weaker straights (e.g., 9x for 6-7-8-9-10 no), and floats. Pot 500 OOP first to act after mutual turn check; betting extracts value from her wide limp-call range (pairs, stubborn draws, traps) while denying equity to any villainous rivers. Sizing 2/3 pot (325) balances value from medium strength vs fold equity from air; larger risks folds, smaller underrepresents. Deep stacks (me 297bb, her 97bb) support bet; checking risks her checking back showdown or small bet we can raise. Value bet maximizes EV.

The river 9 completes my straight. With Td3d on 8c-6h-7c-Js-9h my best five is 7-8-9-10-J (a very strong made straight). Elon’s small river bet (325 into the pot, pot shown 825) looks like a thin value/blocking bet or a small probe — he’s likely to have a one-pair hand (Jx, 9x, 8x) or a missed draw that will call a sizable raise. Calling 325 just concedes value; raising for value is the +EV play. I’ll raise to extract calls from worse one-pair hands and thin value hands but won’t jam and scare off thin callers. A raise to 2000 (well above the min-raise of 650) is large enough to charge Jx/9x/8x and many bluffs while keeping plenty of stack behind. If Elon shoves over it I’ll be calling — my straight is extremely strong here.
