River facing Minni's raise to 2000 (call 1675 into 2825 pot, ~2.7:1 or 27% needed) with 4d5d for the 4-5-6-7-8 straight (tops at 8). Strong but vulnerable to superior straights: 5x (5-6-7-8-9 top 9), Tx9/T8s (6-7-8-9-T top T), JTx/J9 (7-8-9-T-J top J), plus sets (66/77/88) or trips (JJ unlikely). Her SB limp-call of flop c-bet on draw-heavy 6-7-8cc (clubs/straight draws) then check-turn, raise-river sizing over thin value bet indicates polarized strong value over bluffs; missed clubs unlikely to oversized raise after mutual check turn. Range favors monsters fitting history (aggro but trapping here IP). Pot odds insufficient vs ~10-20% bluff %; fold preserves deep stack (294bb) for 43 hands left vs shallow Minni (77bb left). Maximize EV long-term.

Heads-up on the button/SB with 42o, a trash hand with no connectivity, suitedness, or high cards. Equity is poor post-flop OOP against BB's wide defending range. Pot odds are 150:50 (3:1), but playability is awful; folding preserves stack for better spots in tournament with deep effective stacks (Minni ~100BB). Standard heads-up SB range folds low offsuit gappers like 42o. No need to bluff-raise or complete.

Heads-up on the button (I’m the small blind) with Ks-3c. In heads-up play Kx hands are fine to open — I want initiative and fold equity while keeping a standard sizing. The pot is 150 and To Call is 50, so I’ll raise to 300 total (about 3x the big blind). This is a standard exploitative open: it pressures the BB, preserves plenty of my ~105 BB stack for postflop play, and lets me play in position. If Elon 3-bets large I’ll fold this hand; if he calls I’ll use position to c-bet favorable boards or control the pot when behind.

Heads-up pre-flop in BB vs aggressive Button/SB raiser Minni (10k stack). 4h5h is a strong speculative hand suited connectors with good playability, straight/flush potential, and solid equity (~35-40% vs typical button open range based on history of her frequent raises). Pot odds excellent: calling 200 into 400 (3:1 direct, plus implied odds deep). Her range wide (often 40-60% from BTN). Folding too tight heads-up; raising risks bloating pot OOP with marginal hand. Call to realize equity post-flop where we can play profitably.

Flop Qc7d4c gives me middle pair (fours) with 5 kicker, backdoor heart flush draw, and minor straight potential. Board has two clubs, making it somewhat drawy, but my hand has decent showdown value and playability heads-up against Minni's wide button range. OOP, pot control is key with a marginal holding vulnerable to overcards, sets, and draws. Betting risks bloating the pot and facing raises from her strong hands or floats; checking realizes equity, invites bluffs from her missed range, and keeps the pot small for turns where I can improve or control. Her aggression post-flop noted in history supports check-call or check-fold depending on sizing.

Heads-up on the button I opened preflop and Elon just checked the Qc-7d-4c flop. I hold Ks-3c: no pair but a strong overcard to the board plus a backdoor club draw. Elon’s check indicates weakness or a wide, unmade calling range (small pairs, missed broadways, some Kx/Qx). This is a good spot to seize initiative and use fold equity. I’ll make a standard half-pot continuation bet (300) to fold many missed hands and keep the pot manageable if called. Plan: if he folds I take the pot; if he calls I re-evaluate on the turn (press with a K or clubs, pot-control or fold to strong raises if I remain K-high).

Heads-up on flop OOP with bottom pair (4s), 5 kicker, gutshot straight draw to 6 (for 4-5-6-7), backdoor heart flush draw, and backdoor club flush draw potential. Board Qc7d4c is somewhat coordinated with two clubs and straight draw possibilities (5-6, 3-5, 8-J etc.), but my hand has solid showdown value and playability vs Minni's wide button continuation betting range (she frequently bets flops lightly post her opens, as seen in hands like 69,71,72,73,77,81,84,87,88,91,95,98,100,104,105). Pot 900, facing 300 bet (pot odds ~4:1 direct, plus implied odds with my stack 29k effective to her 10k). Folding is too tight; equity likely 40%+ vs her polarized range (value: Qx,77,44, sets; bluffs: Ax, Kx, broadways, missed draws). Raising risks bloating pot OOP with marginal hand facing her aggression (she re-raises light sometimes, e.g., hand 107). Check-call realizes equity cheaply, invites more bluffs on turn/river, controls pot size for unimproved turns. Plan: call, evaluate turn; fold to big bets if blank or improve to re-eval.

Heads-up on turn OOP with bottom pair (4s), weak kicker (5), gutshot straight draw to 6 (for 4-5-6-7 straight), backdoor heart flush draw. Board Qc7d4c9c is extremely draw-heavy: three clubs (nut flush draw heavy), potential straight draws (5-6-8-10, 8-10-J, 10-J-K etc.), pairs 9s, Qs now stronger. My hand has showdown value but is vulnerable to any Q,7,9,A,K, sets, two-pair, flushes, and better pairs/draws. Pot 1200, effective stacks ~10k (her 10k, me 28k). Minni's wide button range (frequent opens/cbets as per history: hands 69-107 show consistent light aggression post-open). She cbets flops lightly, turns selectively. Betting OOP risks: (1) getting raised/folded out by her value (Qx,77,44,99, Q9 etc.); (2) bloating pot OOP with marginal equity (~30-40% vs her range, less vs polarized turn bets); (3) facing floats turning into value. Check is optimal: pot control OOP, realize equity cheaply vs her frequent bluff-bets (history of turn bets on missed boards), induces bluffs from missed draws/broadways, keeps pot manageable for river decisions. If she checks behind, free river card good for gutshot/backdoors. If bets small, call viable; big bet, fold. Raising overkill/fold equity not needed with playability. Plan: check, call reasonable bets or check-fold bombs.

Turn: Q♣ 7♦ 4♣ 9♣. I hold K♠3♣ — no pair but a club in hand gives me a one-card flush draw (board has three clubs now) and a K overcard. My immediate equity if called is roughly: 9 clubs + 3 kings - 1 double-counted K♣ ≈ 11 outs (~24% to improve on the river). Elon’s line (call preflop, check-call my flop c-bet, check turn) looks like a wide/marginal calling range (Qx, small pairs, single-club draws) showing some weakness. This is a good spot to seize the initiative with fold equity while keeping the pot manageable if called. I’ll make a half-pot turn bet (600). Size rationale: large enough to fold out many marginal pairs and Qx hands, but small enough to call a reasonable raise with a draw or to fold to a strong reraise rather than commit with only K-high. If he folds I scoop the 1,200 pot; if he calls I still have ~24% to improve and can re-evaluate on the river (bet if I hit or most rivers check-control / fold to heavy action if I miss). If he check-raises big I will generally release this hand.

Heads-up OOP on turn with bottom pair (4s), gutshot straight draw to 6 (4 outs), backdoor heart draw on scary board Qc7d4c9c (flush-heavy with three clubs, straight draws like T8, 56, 89 possible). Pot 1800, facing 600 bet (~33% pot) from aggressive button Minni (9.4k stack), whose history shows frequent light c-bets and turn barrels (e.g., hands 69-107). Her range polarized: value (Qx,77,99, sets, Q9s/T8s flush draws/combos); bluffs (Ax/Kx broadway, missed clubs, air). Equity ~30-40% vs this range (pots odds 3:1 require ~25%), plus implied odds (her shorter stack but my deep). Folding too tight given history and playability; raising OOP bloats pot/risks vs value. Call realizes equity cheaply, invites river bluffs, controls pot for river OOP (fold unimproved blanks/scares, bet/fold/raise if improves to two-pair/straight/flush). Consistent with prior hand plan.

Heads-up on river OOP in BB vs aggressive button Minni (9.4k effective stack). Holding bottom pair 4s with 5 kicker on paired 9s board (Qc7d4c9c9h), which is draw-heavy (missed clubs, hearts now pair), vulnerable to any 9, Qx, 7x, sets, two-pair, or better pairs. Equity ~25-35% vs her polarized range (value: 99/Q9/Qx/77/44/sets/combos; bluffs: Ax/Kx/broadways/missed draws from wide button open). Pot 2400. Betting OOP is disastrous: folds out worse, gets raised by value, no fold equity needed. Check pot-controls, induces bluffs from her frequent river bets (history: light aggression in 69-108, e.g., turn/river barrels on scary boards), allows cheap realization of equity showdown value (bottom pair often good vs bluffs), preserves stack late tournament (109/150, her short). If she bets small (<1200), call viable; large bet fold. Consistent with check-call strategy entire hand.

River: board Qc 7d 4c 9c 9h; I hold K♠3♣ — no pair, the river missed my flush draw. Elon check-called my 300 flop and 600 turn bets and now checks the river: that line is consistent with a wide/marginal calling range (Qx, small pocket pairs, single-club combos or missed hands) rather than a strong value hand. I have essentially no showdown value, so checking just concedes the pot to his marginal hands. I will bluff to try to fold out those one-pair hands and convert the pot. I choose a ~2/3-pot bet of 1,600. Sizing rationale: large enough to credibly represent a made club or a strong Q/K and to fold out many Qx / 7x / 4x hands that called earlier, but small enough that I can fold to a sizeable check-raise without overcommitting. Break-even fold frequency for this bet is about 40% (1600/(2400+1600)). Given his passive check-call line I estimate his fold rate here is in that neighborhood, so this is +EV. If Elon folds I scoop the 2,400 pot; if he calls I lose the bluff; if he check-raises big/all-in I will fold (K-high has no showdown value vs large aggression).

River OOP heads-up vs aggressive button Minni (wide opening range ~40-50% historically from btn/sb, frequent light c-bets/turn/river barrels on drawy boards as seen hands 69-108 e.g. 83,87,88,90,91,107). Board Qc7d4c9c9h pairs 9s, missed club flush draw (3 clubs), possible missed straight draws (T8,56 for open-enders earlier), favors her polarized range: value (99/trips, Q9/Qc/Q7,77/sets QQ/44 rare); bluffs (A-high/K-high/broadway, missed clubs/Ax floats, air). Holding bottom pair 4h (pairs 4c) +5h kicker beats all bluffs/worse pairs/draws (~40%+ equity vs range), loses to value. Pot 4000, call 1600 (~40% pot bet, pot odds 1600 into 5600 = ~22% needed). Excellent odds for showdown value vs her bluff-heavy history (e.g. river folds to checks or light bets). Consistent OOP check-call station plan entire hand realizing equity cheaply; folding too tight exploits her overbluffing. Raising unnecessary (no FE needed, stacks deep post-call her 7835 behind minimal implied reverse). Call preserves stack late (hand 109/150), extracts value if behind (rare) or wins vs bluffs. Plan post-call: showdown.

Heads-up on the button/SB vs BB with Qs5h (offsuit queen-high). Folding any queen-high is far too tight in heads-up play. The hand has decent playability, position, and fold equity against Minni's BB defending range. My stack is over 4x her effective stack (32k vs 7.7k), so applying pressure with a wide raising range maximizes EV by stealing blinds frequently and punishing her shorter stack. She defends/3-bets reasonably wide based on history, but fold equity is still high. Standard raise size: 3x the BB (300 total) balances value, bluffs, and pot control.

Heads-up vs Elon who opened to 300 from the SB. I’m the BB with Th6h — suited but a marginal hand and I’ll be out of position postflop. Pot is 400 and calling 200 more (300 total committed) gives ~25% break-even equity; T6s has reasonable equity vs a wide HU open and plays well on flops with backdoor hearts. I don’t want to 3-bet and build a bloated pot OOP with a weak hand, so I’ll call to see a flop and proceed cautiously: continue only with clear equity (pair, strong draw) and fold to heavy pressure on dry boards.

Flop is 2♦–3♦–4♣ and my hand is T♥6♥. I missed — no pair and no flush draw (there are no hearts on board). I have an inside (gutshot) straight to any 5 (4 outs) — ~8.5% to hit on the turn, ~16.5% by the river — and 3 tens that could pair but often will be second-best. Pot is small (600) relative to my stack (7,535) and Elon has been opening/playing aggressively; I don’t want to build a big OOP pot with only a 4‑out draw. I’ll check to control the pot and see the turn. Plan: if Elon checks back I can take a free card; if he bets a small C‑bet (~≤1/3 pot) I’ll call to realize equity, but I’ll fold to larger bets/raises to avoid committing chips with marginal equity.

Heads-up on the button vs BB after my preflop raise and her call. Flop 2d 3d 4c is highly coordinated: flush draw, open-enders (56, A5 wheel), gutshots everywhere. My Qs5h is queen-high with a gutshot straight draw to the 6 (4 outs, ~8% equity), backdoor spade flush draw, and blocks queens and some straight draws (5x). Queen-high has showdown value against her capping range. She checks, which is standard from BB OOP but polarizes slightly weaker. In position as preflop aggressor, wide c-bet range (60-80%) is +EV here to deny equity to her draws, protect my hand, charge, and leverage fold equity given her history of folding to flop aggression (e.g., Hands 100, 84, 92). Pot 600, her effective stack 7535 (~12.5x pot). Bet sizing 50% pot (300) balances value (overpairs, sets, made straights), bluffs (my range), and thin value while keeping SPR manageable postflop. Larger sizes risk check-raise all-in from draws; smaller risks floats. Maximizes EV long-term in tournament.

I’m OOP (BB) with T♥6♥ on 2♦-3♦-4♣ — I completely missed: no pair and no diamond draw. I have a gutshot to any 5 (4 outs) plus three tens (7 outs total) — roughly ~15% to hit on the turn and ~28% by the river. Elon opened and now made a small c-bet (300 into the pot), which gives me ~3:1 pot odds (break-even ~25%). Given the opponent’s wide, aggressive opening range on a dry, low board, calling to realize equity is +EV here while keeping the pot controlled. Plan: call 300 to see the turn; fold to significant turn aggression (big bet/raise) since my equity is marginal, but if I hit a 5 or a T I’ll start extracting value/raise as appropriate.

Elon checked the turn into a 1,200 pot and I hold T♥6♥ on 2♦–3♦–4♣–9♥. I have no made hand and no realistic flush (runner‑runner only), with 10 river outs (4 fives + 3 tens + 3 sixes) ≈19.6% to improve. Betting OOP here would bloat a pot I’m not happy to play big in with a marginal hand — many hands that call a bet (overcards, small pairs) still beat a single pair, and Elon’s shown tendency to play aggressively means he’ll often continue. Stack sizes also favor pot control. I’ll check to see a free river and preserve fold equity on the river; if Elon barrels the river large I’ll fold without improving, but I’ll value-bet or call when I pair the river.

Heads-up on button vs BB turn after c-bet flop call. Board 2d3d4c9h remains draw-heavy (diamond flush draw, wheel straight draws, now 9h blank for most). Qs5h has queen-high showdown value, gutshot blocker (5 blocks A5 wheel), and position. Minni's check-check suggests weakness: missed draws (e.g., AdXd, 56s), weak pairs that fear my range. Her history shows frequent folds to flop/turn aggression (e.g., Hands 84, 92, 100, 107). Pot 1200, her 7235 (~6x pot), my deep stack allows pressure without risk. Betting continues preflop aggression, denies equity to remaining draws, extracts from Ax, weaker Qx, pairs. Check risks giving free river cards or check-raise, while bet size ~75% pot (900) builds pot for river value, applies fold equity (high vs her short stack), keeps SPR low (~5:1 post-call). Balances vs her calling range (sets, two-pair rare). +EV vs passive defender.

Fold. Elon barreled the turn for 900 into what was ~1,200 (pot now 2,100). Calling 900 to win a 3,000 pot requires ~30% equity (900/3000). With T♥6♥ on 2♦–3♦–4♣–9♥ I only have 10 clean river outs (4 fives + 3 tens + 3 sixes) ≈21–22% to improve — well below the break-even threshold. I’m out of position on a very dry board and Elon’s double-barrel polarizes his range toward value or a shove/call line that I can’t profitably continue against. A raise as a bluff is too high-variance given his deep stack and my lack of fold equity. Following my earlier plan to check the turn and fold to large turn aggression, I’ll fold and preserve my stack for a better spot.

Heads-up on the button with Kc Qh — a very playable, top-tier hand in position. The pot is 150 and it’s only 50 to call, but I want to seize initiative, isolate the BB’s wide defending range, and build the pot with position. With ~72BB I can comfortably raise; sizing to 300 total (~3x the BB) is a standard constructive raise: it gives good fold equity, gets value from worse hands (Kx, Qx, Ax) and leaves plenty of stack for postflop play. Plan is to continue postflop if called, and to re-evaluate vs large 3-bets based on sizing and opponent tendencies.

Heads-up against Minni on the button/SB with a wide raising range given her history of frequent preflop aggression. My hand K♥J♥ is premium suited broadway, crushing much of her range (e.g., Ax suited, broadways, suited connectors). Positionally disadvantaged postflop as BB, so 3-betting builds the pot when ahead, applies pressure on her shorter stack (7k effective), and takes down the pot often preflop. Standard 3x sizing to 900 total bet provides good fold equity while keeping her in with worse. Calling is fine but misses value/equity denial; folding is never correct.

Elon 3-bet to 900 after my 300 open. I’m on the button with KcQh and ~69BB effective vs a much deeper opponent. KQo heads-up in position is a very playable hand — it has good equity vs a wide 3-bet range and benefits from position postflop. Calling the additional 600 (900 total) keeps the pot reasonable, preserves my positional advantage, and lets me realize equity or apply pressure on flops I connect with. A 4-bet to 1500 is unnecessary with KQo here (it bloats the pot against a deeper, capable opponent and commits more without a clear fold equity edge). Plan: call, evaluate flop — continue aggressively with top pair/strong draws, use position to C-bet favorable boards, and fold or pot-control vs heavy resistance on bad runouts.

Flop 2c Jd 5h hits my K♥ J♥ perfectly: top pair top kicker (Jacks) with nut flush draw (9 outs) and backdoor straight potential. Equity crushes Minni's calling range (Ax, broadways, suited connectors, pairs below KK). As preflop 3-bettor OOP, c-betting denies her equity on a coordinated board while building pot with massive showdown/value. Her button call pre suggests wide range including draws, mid pairs, floats. Half-pot sizing (1200 into 1800) balances protection/value/fold equity vs her short stack (6.3k), keeping her committed with worse while avoiding overbet jam risk.

Call 1200. Heads-up in position with KcQh: I’ve missed the flop but hold two clean overcards and a small backdoor club/runner potential. Versus Elon's 3‑bet/c‑bet range my chance to pair by the river is ~24% and backdoor club/other outs add another ~5–6% — roughly ~29% total equity. Pot odds on a 1200 call into a 3000 pot (~4200 final) require ~28.6% equity, so this is a marginal +EV spot where position matters. Calling keeps the pot under control, lets me realize equity and use position to apply pressure if he shows weakness or I improve; I’ll re‑evaluate on the turn (fold to heavy turn aggression if I don’t improve, raise/bet if I pair or pick up strong draws).

Turn brings the Ks, pairing my Kh for top pair (now TPTK improved to two pair Kings and Jacks with the Jd and Jh) plus the nut heart flush draw (9 outs) and backdoor straight outs. Monster hand that crushes Minni's flop calling range (AJ, KQ, QJ, JTs, mid pairs, sets <JJ rare). Board 2c-Jd-5h-Ks has straight draw potential (QTs) and backdoor flush, but Ks is a bluff catcher card for her floats/draws while massively improving me. Pot 4200, her effective stack 5135 (SPR ~1.2), she's pot-committed. OOP as 3bet pre/c-bet flop, continue with large value bet to extract from worse (Ax suited, broadways w/o K, Jx) while denying equity on scary rivers (hearts, Q for str8). ~3/4 pot sizing (3000) balances max value vs fold equity; smaller risks cheap river showdown, all-in risks folding AJ/QJ. Her history shows calling stations on value lines (e.g. Hands 15,37,45), so expect calls w/ worse. Maximizes stack EV long-term.

I called the flop and the turn K paired my hand — I now have top pair with a Q kicker. Elon has shown a strong, value‑heavy line (3‑bet preflop, c‑bet flop, big turn bet to 3,000), but the call requires ~29% equity (3,000 into a 7,200 pot) and KQ on the turn has reasonable equity against a wide continuing range (and beats a lot of his flop‑pairs and weaker Kx). The minimum raise would have to be an all‑in for 5,135; shoving would commit most of my stack and be -EV against hands that dominate me (AK/AA/QQ) while only folding out a subset of worse hands. Calling keeps the pot controllable, lets me realize equity and re‑evaluate on the river (fold if he barrels heavily and I don’t improve, extract value if he shows weakness). Call 3,000.

River 4h completes the nut heart flush for me (Kh Jh with board 5h 4h), making the absolute nuts barring her exact Ahxh combos which are unlikely given preflop action and her calling range. Additionally, two pair KKJJ as backup. Board has flush, possible wheel straight (A3 unlikely), but crushes her range for calling pre 3bet, flop cbet on Jd-high coordinated board, and large turn bet after Ks: likely AJ/KJ/QJ/JT/TT/55/pocket pairs/draws that called OOP. Pot 10200, her remaining stack 2135 (SPR ~0.21), she's pot-committed. History shows Minni as a calling station on value lines (e.g., Hands 15,37,45,56,81,109). OOP first to act: jamming maximizes value from worse flushes (QhJh+, lower), top pair/good kicker, sets (JJ/55/KK rare), overpairs. Checking risks free showdown or her thin value; smaller bet risks raise/fold equity loss. Full pot commitment extracts max EV for tournament stack growth.

Call 2135. The turn paired my king, so I have top pair (K♣) with a decent kicker (Q♥) on 2♣ J♦ 5♥ K♠ 4♥. Elon's line (preflop 3‑bet, flop continuation bet, large turn bet) is value‑heavy but also contains many hands (JJ, 55, AQ/AJ, some weaker Kx) that are now behind me after the K. The river bet is small relative to the pot: 2,135 into 12,335, so I need only ~2135/(12335+2135) ≈ 14.8% equity to make a correct call. Given the range that continues to this river, KQ wins far more than that frequency. Shoving would commit my stack and be -EV versus the hands that dominate me (AK/AA/QQ); folding throws away too much equity. I call and show down my top pair.
